papers.title: Lost in Stories: Consistency Bugs in Long Story Generation by LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language models struggle with maintaining narrative consistency over long-form storytelling, exhibiting predictable patterns of contradictions that this benchmark identifies and categorizes.  					AI-generated summary What happens when a storyteller forgets its own story? Large Language Models (LLMs) can now generate narratives spanning tens of thousands of words, but they often fail to maintain consistency throughout. When generating long-form narratives, these models can contradict their own established facts, character traits, and world rules. Existing story generation benchmarks focus mainly on plot quality and fluency, leaving consistency errors largely unexplored. To address this gap, we present ConStory-Bench, a benchmark designed to evaluate narrative consistency in long-form story generation. It contains 2,000 prompts across four task scenarios and defines a taxonomy of five error categories with 19 fine-grained subtypes. We also develop ConStory-Checker, an automated pipeline that detects contradictions and grounds each judgment in explicit textual evidence. Evaluating a range of LLMs through five research questions, we find that consistency errors show clear tendencies: they are most common in factual and temporal dimensions, tend to appear around the middle of narratives, occur in text segments with higher token-level entropy, and certain error types tend to co-occur. These findings can inform future efforts to improve consistency in long-form narrative generation. Our project page is available at https://picrew.github.io/constory-bench.github.io/.
affiliations: Singapore University of Technology and Design

ru.title: Когда рассказчик забывает свою историю: проблема согласованности в генерации длинных нарративов
ru.desc: В статье представлена ConStory-Bench — новый бенчмарк для оценки согласованности повествования в длинных текстах, генерируемых большими языковыми моделями. Авторы выявили, что LLM часто противоречат сами себе в фактических и временных деталях, персонажах и правилах вымышленного мира при создании длинных нарративов. Разработана автоматическая система ConStory-Checker, которая обнаруживает противоречия с помощью явных текстовых доказательств и классифицирует ошибки по 19 подтипам. Исследование показало, что ошибки последовательности чаще всего появляются в середине текста и связаны с более высокой энтропией токенов.

en.title: Ensuring Consistency in AI Storytelling
en.desc: This paper addresses the issue of narrative consistency in long-form storytelling generated by Large Language Models (LLMs). It introduces ConStory-Bench, a benchmark that evaluates how well these models maintain consistency throughout their narratives. The benchmark categorizes consistency errors into five main types and provides a tool, ConStory-Checker, to automatically detect these contradictions. The study reveals patterns in these errors, highlighting their prevalence in specific narrative contexts and suggesting areas for improvement in LLM storytelling.

zh.title: 提升长篇叙事一致性的关键
zh.desc: 大型语言模型在生成长篇故事时，常常难以保持叙事的一致性，容易出现自相矛盾的情况。本文提出了ConStory-Bench，一个专门评估长篇故事生成中叙事一致性的基准，包含2000个提示和五种错误类型的分类。我们还开发了ConStory-Checker，一个自动化工具，用于检测矛盾并提供明确的文本证据支持判断。研究发现，一致性错误在事实和时间维度上最为常见，且通常出现在叙事的中间部分。

papers.title: Holi-Spatial: Evolving Video Streams into Holistic 3D Spatial Intelligence
papers.abstract: Abstract Holi-Spatial presents the first fully automated, large-scale, spatially-aware multimodal dataset constructed from raw video inputs, supporting multi-level spatial supervision for 3D scene understanding and spatial reasoning tasks.  					AI-generated summary The pursuit of spatial intelligence fundamentally relies on access to large-scale, fine-grained 3D data. However, existing approaches predominantly construct spatial understanding benchmarks by generating question-answer (QA) pairs from a limited number of manually annotated datasets, rather than systematically annotating new large-scale 3D scenes from raw web data. As a result, their scalability is severely constrained, and model performance is further hindered by domain gaps inherent in these narrowly curated datasets.   In this work, we propose Holi-Spatial, the first fully automated, large-scale, spatially-aware multimodal dataset, constructed from raw video inputs without human intervention, using the proposed data curation pipeline. Holi-Spatial supports multi-level spatial supervision, ranging from geometrically accurate 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) reconstructions with rendered depth maps to object-level and relational semantic annotations, together with corresponding spatial Question-Answer (QA) pairs.   Following a principled and systematic pipeline, we further construct Holi-Spatial-4M, the first large-scale, high-quality 3D semantic dataset, containing 12K optimized 3DGS scenes, 1.3M 2D masks, 320K 3D bounding boxes, 320K instance captions, 1.2M 3D grounding instances, and 1.2M spatial QA pairs spanning diverse geometric, relational, and semantic reasoning tasks.   Holi-Spatial demonstrates exceptional performance in data curation quality, significantly outperforming existing feed-forward and per-scene optimized methods on datasets such as ScanNet, ScanNet++, and DL3DV. Furthermore, fine-tuning Vision-Language Models (VLMs) on spatial reasoning tasks using this dataset has also led to substantial improvements in model performance.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Автоматическая генерация масштабного датасета для пространственного понимания 3D сцен из видео
ru.desc: Авторы представили Holi-Spatial — первый полностью автоматизированный датасет большого масштаба для понимания 3D сцен, построенный из сырых видеоданных без человеческого участия. Датасет содержит многоуровневую пространственную разметку: реконструкции сцен с помощью 3D Gaussian Splatting, карты глубины, маски объектов и семантические аннотации. Holi-Spatial-4M включает 12K оптимизированных 3D сцен с 1,2M пар вопрос-ответ для пространственного рассуждения, что позволяет решать задачи геометрического, реляционного и семантического анализа. Fine-tuning моделей Vision-Language на этом датасете показал значительное улучшение производительности и превосходит существующие бенчмарки по качеству разметки.

en.title: Holi-Spatial: Revolutionizing 3D Scene Understanding with Automated Data
en.desc: Holi-Spatial introduces a groundbreaking automated dataset designed for 3D scene understanding and spatial reasoning, created from raw video inputs without human intervention. This dataset features multi-level spatial supervision, including accurate 3D reconstructions and detailed semantic annotations, which enhance the training of machine learning models. By addressing the limitations of existing datasets, Holi-Spatial provides a scalable solution that significantly improves model performance on various spatial reasoning tasks. The dataset's extensive features, including millions of 3D instances and spatial question-answer pairs, set a new standard for future research in spatial intelligence.

zh.title: Holi-Spatial：自动化构建的大规模空间感知数据集
zh.desc: Holi-Spatial是首个完全自动化的大规模空间感知多模态数据集，基于原始视频输入构建，支持多层次空间监督，旨在提升3D场景理解和空间推理任务的能力。该数据集通过无人工干预的方式，采用系统化的数据整理流程，生成几何准确的3D重建和相应的语义注释。Holi-Spatial-4M包含12000个优化的3D场景和大量的2D掩码、3D边界框及空间问答对，覆盖多种几何、关系和语义推理任务。通过在空间推理任务上微调视觉-语言模型，使用该数据集显著提升了模型性能。

papers.title: LoGeR: Long-Context Geometric Reconstruction with Hybrid Memory
papers.abstract: Abstract LoGeR enables long-term 3D video reconstruction by combining bidirectional priors with a hybrid memory system that includes parametric Test-Time Training and non-parametric sliding window attention mechanisms.  					AI-generated summary Feedforward geometric foundation models achieve strong short-window reconstruction, yet scaling them to minutes-long videos is bottlenecked by quadratic attention complexity or limited effective memory in recurrent designs. We present LoGeR (Long-context Geometric Reconstruction), a novel architecture that scales dense 3D reconstruction to extremely long sequences without post-optimization. LoGeR processes video streams in chunks, leveraging strong bidirectional priors for high-fidelity intra-chunk reasoning. To manage the critical challenge of coherence across chunk boundaries, we propose a learning-based hybrid memory module. This dual-component system combines a parametric Test-Time Training (TTT) memory to anchor the global coordinate frame and prevent scale drift, alongside a non-parametric Sliding Window Attention (SWA) mechanism to preserve uncompressed context for high-precision adjacent alignment. Remarkably, this memory architecture enables LoGeR to be trained on sequences of 128 frames, and generalize up to thousands of frames during inference. Evaluated across standard benchmarks and a newly repurposed VBR dataset with sequences of up to 19k frames, LoGeR substantially outperforms prior state-of-the-art feedforward methods--reducing ATE on KITTI by over 74%--and achieves robust, globally consistent reconstruction over unprecedented horizons.

ru.title: Масштабируемая геометрическая реконструкция видео с гибридной памятью
ru.desc: LoGeR — это архитектура глубокого обучения для восстановления трёхмерной геометрии из длинных видеопоследовательностей. Модель решает проблему масштабируемости, обрабатывая видео блоками с использованием двусторонних приоров для качественного локального анализа. Гибридная система памяти сочетает параметрическое Test-Time Training для стабилизации глобальной системы координат и скользящее оконное внимание для сохранения точного выравнивания между блоками. Система обучается на коротких последовательностях (128 кадров), но обобщается на тысячи кадров при инференсе, достигая беспрецедентной точности и консистентности трёхмерной реконструкции.

en.title: Revolutionizing Long-Term 3D Video Reconstruction with LoGeR
en.desc: LoGeR is a new machine learning architecture designed for long-term 3D video reconstruction. It combines bidirectional priors with a hybrid memory system that includes parametric Test-Time Training and non-parametric sliding window attention mechanisms. This innovative approach allows LoGeR to effectively process long video sequences without the need for post-optimization, achieving high fidelity in reconstruction. The model has shown significant improvements over previous methods, particularly in maintaining coherence across chunk boundaries and generalizing to thousands of frames during inference.

zh.title: LoGeR：长时序3D视频重建的新突破
zh.desc: LoGeR是一种新型的长时序3D视频重建架构，结合了双向先验和混合记忆系统。它通过处理视频流的块，利用强大的双向先验进行高保真度的块内推理。为了应对块边界之间的一致性挑战，LoGeR引入了一种学习型混合记忆模块，结合了参数化的测试时训练和非参数的滑动窗口注意力机制。经过评估，LoGeR在多个标准基准测试中表现优异，能够在推理时处理长达数千帧的序列。

papers.title: Believe Your Model: Distribution-Guided Confidence Calibration
papers.abstract: Abstract Large reasoning models enhance prediction accuracy through test-time scaling techniques that generate multiple candidate responses, with the proposed DistriVoting method utilizing distributional priors and confidence scores to improve answer selection.  					AI-generated summary Large Reasoning Models have demonstrated remarkable performance with the advancement of test-time scaling techniques, which enhances prediction accuracy by generating multiple candidate responses and selecting the most reliable answer. While prior work has analyzed that internal model signals like confidence scores can partly indicate response correctness and exhibit a distributional correlation with accuracy, such distributional information has not been fully utilized to guide answer selection. Motivated by this, we propose DistriVoting, which incorporates distributional priors as another signal alongside confidence during voting. Specifically, our method (1) first decomposes the mixed confidence distribution into positive and negative components using Gaussian Mixture Models, (2) then applies a reject filter based on positive/negative samples from them to mitigate overlap between the two distributions. Besides, to further alleviate the overlap from the perspective of distribution itself, we propose SelfStepConf, which uses step-level confidence to dynamically adjust inference process, increasing the separation between the two distributions to improve the reliability of confidences in voting. Experiments across 16 models and 5 benchmarks demonstrate that our method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art approaches.

ru.title: Голосование с учетом распределения уверенности для надежного выбора ответов
ru.desc: Статья посвящена улучшению точности предсказаний больших языковых моделей на этапе вывода через масштабирование вычислений. Авторы предлагают метод DistriVoting, который использует распределительные приоры и оценки уверенности модели для более надежного выбора среди нескольких сгенерированных ответов. Метод разлагает смешанное распределение уверенности на положительные и отрицательные компоненты с помощью смесей Гауссовых распределений и применяет фильтр отклонения для уменьшения пересечения между ними. Дополнительно предлагается метод SelfStepConf, который использует уверенность на уровне шагов рассуждения для динамической адаптации процесса вывода и повышения надежности голосования.

en.title: DistriVoting: Enhancing Answer Selection with Distributional Insights
en.desc: This paper introduces DistriVoting, a novel method that enhances the accuracy of large reasoning models by effectively utilizing distributional priors and confidence scores during answer selection. The approach involves decomposing confidence distributions into positive and negative components using Gaussian Mixture Models, which helps in identifying reliable responses. Additionally, a reject filter is applied to reduce overlap between these distributions, ensuring clearer distinctions in confidence levels. The proposed SelfStepConf further refines the inference process by dynamically adjusting confidence, leading to improved performance across multiple models and benchmarks.

zh.title: 利用分布先验提升答案选择准确性
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为DistriVoting的方法，旨在通过利用分布先验和置信度来提高答案选择的准确性。该方法首先使用高斯混合模型将混合置信度分布分解为正负成分，然后应用拒绝过滤器来减少这两种分布之间的重叠。此外，DistriVoting还引入了SelfStepConf，通过动态调整推理过程中的置信度，进一步提高了分布的分离度。实验结果表明，该方法在16个模型和5个基准测试中显著优于现有的最先进方法。

papers.title: How Far Can Unsupervised RLVR Scale LLM Training?
papers.abstract: Abstract Unsupervised reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards faces fundamental limitations in scaling large language model training due to inherent convergence properties and confidence-correction misalignment, though external methods show promise for overcoming these barriers.  					AI-generated summary Unsupervised reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (URLVR) offers a pathway to scale LLM training beyond the supervision bottleneck by deriving rewards without ground truth labels. Recent works leverage model intrinsic signals, showing promising early gains, yet their potential and limitations remain unclear. In this work, we revisit URLVR and provide a comprehensive analysis spanning taxonomy, theory and extensive experiments. We first classify URLVR methods into intrinsic versus external based on reward sources, then establish a unified theoretical framework revealing that all intrinsic methods converge toward sharpening the model's initial distribution This sharpening mechanism succeeds when initial confidence aligns with correctness but fails catastrophically when misaligned. Through systematic experiments, we show intrinsic rewards consistently follow a rise-then-fall pattern across methods, with collapse timing determined by model prior rather than engineering choices. Despite these scaling limits, we find intrinsic rewards remain valuable in test-time training on small datasets, and propose Model Collapse Step to measure model prior, serving as a practical indicator for RL trainability. Finally, we explore external reward methods that ground verification in computational asymmetries, showing preliminary evidence they may escape the confidence-correctness ceiling. Our findings chart boundaries for intrinsic URLVR while motivating paths toward scalable alternatives.

ru.title: Границы внутренних вознаграждений: путь к масштабируемому обучению с подкреплением
ru.desc: В работе анализируется неконтролируемое обучение с подкреплением (URLVR) с проверяемыми вознаграждениями для масштабирования обучения большых языковых моделей. Авторы показывают, что все методы на основе внутренних сигналов модели сходятся к повышению остроты начального распределения вероятностей, что приводит к катастрофическому коллапсу при несовпадении уверенности модели с корректностью. Исследование демонстрирует универсальный паттерн роста-падения эффективности вознаграждений и предлагает метрику Model Collapse Step для оценки предварительного распределения модели. Авторы предполагают, что внешние методы вознаграждений, основанные на вычислительной асимметрии, могут преодолеть выявленные ограничения масштабирования.

en.title: Unlocking Scalable Learning: Overcoming URLVR Challenges
en.desc: This paper investigates unsupervised reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (URLVR) and its challenges in scaling large language model (LLM) training. It categorizes URLVR methods into intrinsic and external based on their reward sources and develops a theoretical framework to understand their convergence properties. The study reveals that intrinsic methods can lead to model collapse when confidence does not align with correctness, while also demonstrating that intrinsic rewards can still be useful in small dataset scenarios. Additionally, the paper explores external reward methods that may help overcome the limitations of intrinsic approaches, suggesting new directions for scalable reinforcement learning.

zh.title: 无监督强化学习的挑战与机遇
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了无监督强化学习（URLVR）在可验证奖励下的局限性，特别是在大规模语言模型训练中的应用。研究表明，内在奖励方法在收敛性和信心校正方面存在根本性问题，导致其在训练中的效果不稳定。作者对URLVR方法进行了分类，并提出了一个统一的理论框架，揭示了内在奖励的收敛机制。尽管存在这些限制，内在奖励在小数据集的测试时训练中仍然具有价值，并提出了模型崩溃步骤作为评估模型先验的实用指标。

papers.title: CARE-Edit: Condition-Aware Routing of Experts for Contextual Image Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract CARE-Edit introduces a condition-aware routing mechanism that dynamically allocates diffusion model computation to specialized experts for improved contextual image editing tasks.  					AI-generated summary Unified diffusion editors often rely on a fixed, shared backbone for diverse tasks, suffering from task interference and poor adaptation to heterogeneous demands (e.g., local vs global, semantic vs photometric). In particular, prevalent ControlNet and OmniControl variants combine multiple conditioning signals (e.g., text, mask, reference) via static concatenation or additive adapters which cannot dynamically prioritize or suppress conflicting modalities, thus resulting in artifacts like color bleeding across mask boundaries, identity or style drift, and unpredictable behavior under multi-condition inputs. To address this, we propose Condition-Aware Routing of Experts (CARE-Edit) that aligns model computation with specific editing competencies. At its core, a lightweight latent-attention router assigns encoded diffusion tokens to four specialized experts--Text, Mask, Reference, and Base--based on multi-modal conditions and diffusion timesteps: (i) a Mask Repaint module first refines coarse user-defined masks for precise spatial guidance; (ii) the router applies sparse top-K selection to dynamically allocate computation to the most relevant experts; (iii) a Latent Mixture module subsequently fuses expert outputs, coherently integrating semantic, spatial, and stylistic information to the base images. Experiments validate CARE-Edit's strong performance on contextual editing tasks, including erasure, replacement, text-driven edits, and style transfer. Empirical analysis further reveals task-specific behavior of specialized experts, showcasing the importance of dynamic, condition-aware processing to mitigate multi-condition conflicts.
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Умная маршрутизация экспертов для точного редактирования изображений
ru.desc: CARE-Edit представляет механизм динамической маршрутизации для диффузионных моделей, который направляет вычисления к специализированным экспертам в зависимости от условий редактирования. Система решает проблему конфликтов между несколькими модальностями (текст, маска, стиль), которые приводят к артефактам при использовании фиксированной архитектуры. Легкий маршрутизатор на основе attention выбирает релевантные эксперты для каждого токена, позволяя модели адаптироваться к различным типам задач редактирования. Экспериментально подтверждена эффективность подхода на задачах удаления объектов, замены, текстового редактирования и трансфера стиля.

en.title: Dynamic Expert Routing for Enhanced Image Editing
en.desc: CARE-Edit presents a novel approach to image editing by implementing a condition-aware routing mechanism that assigns tasks to specialized experts based on the context of the editing request. This method addresses the limitations of traditional diffusion models that struggle with task interference and poor adaptability to varying demands. By utilizing a lightweight latent-attention router, CARE-Edit dynamically selects the most relevant expert for each editing condition, improving the quality of outputs and reducing artifacts. The results demonstrate that this targeted allocation of computational resources enhances performance in various editing tasks, such as erasure and style transfer.

zh.title: 动态条件感知路由，提升图像编辑效果
zh.desc: CARE-Edit提出了一种条件感知的路由机制，能够动态分配扩散模型的计算资源给专门的专家，以提高上下文图像编辑任务的效果。传统的统一扩散编辑器依赖固定的共享骨干网络，导致任务干扰和适应性差。CARE-Edit通过轻量级的潜在注意力路由器，将编码的扩散标记分配给四个专门的专家，确保模型计算与特定编辑能力相匹配。实验结果验证了CARE-Edit在上下文编辑任务中的强大性能，展示了动态条件感知处理的重要性。

papers.title: CoCo: Code as CoT for Text-to-Image Preview and Rare Concept Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract CoCo is a code-driven framework for text-to-image generation that uses executable code for precise spatial layout planning and structured image creation, achieving superior performance over traditional CoT-based methods.  					AI-generated summary Recent advancements in Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) have significantly advanced text-to-image (T2I) generation, particularly through the integration of Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning. However, existing CoT-based T2I methods largely rely on abstract natural-language planning, which lacks the precision required for complex spatial layouts, structured visual elements, and dense textual content. In this work, we propose CoCo (Code-as-CoT), a code-driven reasoning framework that represents the reasoning process as executable code, enabling explicit and verifiable intermediate planning for image generation. Given a text prompt, CoCo first generates executable code that specifies the structural layout of the scene, which is then executed in a sandboxed environment to render a deterministic draft image. The model subsequently refines this draft through fine-grained image editing to produce the final high-fidelity result. To support this training paradigm, we construct CoCo-10K, a curated dataset containing structured draft-final image pairs designed to teach both structured draft construction and corrective visual refinement. Empirical evaluations on StructT2IBench, OneIG-Bench, and LongText-Bench show that CoCo achieves improvements of +68.83%, +54.8%, and +41.23% over direct generation, while also outperforming other generation methods empowered by CoT. These results demonstrate that executable code is an effective and reliable reasoning paradigm for precise, controllable, and structured text-to-image generation. The code is available at: https://github.com/micky-li-hd/CoCo
affiliations: Institute of Automation, Chinese Academy of Sciences
The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Код вместо слов: точная генерация изображений через исполняемые программы
ru.desc: CoCo — это фреймворк для генерации изображений по текстовому описанию, который вместо абстрактного рассуждения на естественном языке использует исполняемый код для точного планирования пространственной раскладки. Система сначала генерирует код, определяющий структуру сцены, затем выполняет его в изолированной среде для получения черновика изображения, а после этого уточняет результат через прецизионное редактирование. Для обучения модели была создана специальная датасет CoCo-10K с парами черновик-финальное изображение. Метод показывает значительные улучшения — на 68.83% лучше прямой генерации — и превосходит другие подходы, основанные на цепочке рассуждений.

en.title: Code-Driven Precision in Text-to-Image Generation
en.desc: CoCo is a novel framework for generating images from text that utilizes executable code to enhance spatial layout planning and structured image creation. Unlike traditional methods that depend on abstract reasoning, CoCo allows for precise control over the image generation process by representing reasoning as code. This approach enables the model to create a draft image based on a structured layout, which is then refined to achieve high-quality results. The framework has shown significant performance improvements over existing methods, demonstrating the effectiveness of code-driven reasoning in text-to-image generation.

zh.title: 代码驱动的文本到图像生成新方法
zh.desc: CoCo是一个基于代码的文本到图像生成框架，利用可执行代码进行精确的空间布局规划和结构化图像创建。与传统的基于思维链（CoT）的方法相比，CoCo在生成复杂的空间布局和结构化视觉元素方面表现更优。该框架首先生成指定场景结构布局的可执行代码，然后在沙箱环境中执行该代码以渲染出确定性的草图图像。最后，通过细致的图像编辑对草图进行优化，生成高保真的最终结果。

papers.title: HiAR: Efficient Autoregressive Long Video Generation via Hierarchical Denoising
papers.abstract: Abstract HiAR, a hierarchical autoregressive diffusion framework, improves video generation by conditioning on context at the same noise level and employs forward-KL regularization to maintain temporal continuity and motion diversity.  					AI-generated summary Autoregressive (AR) diffusion offers a promising framework for generating videos of theoretically infinite length. However, a major challenge is maintaining temporal continuity while preventing the progressive quality degradation caused by error accumulation. To ensure continuity, existing methods typically condition on highly denoised contexts; yet, this practice propagates prediction errors with high certainty, thereby exacerbating degradation. In this paper, we argue that a highly clean context is unnecessary. Drawing inspiration from bidirectional diffusion models, which denoise frames at a shared noise level while maintaining coherence, we propose that conditioning on context at the same noise level as the current block provides sufficient signal for temporal consistency while effectively mitigating error propagation. Building on this insight, we propose HiAR, a hierarchical denoising framework that reverses the conventional generation order: instead of completing each block sequentially, it performs causal generation across all blocks at every denoising step, so that each block is always conditioned on context at the same noise level. This hierarchy naturally admits pipelined parallel inference, yielding a 1.8 wall-clock speedup in our 4-step setting. We further observe that self-rollout distillation under this paradigm amplifies a low-motion shortcut inherent to the mode-seeking reverse-KL objective. To counteract this, we introduce a forward-KL regulariser in bidirectional-attention mode, which preserves motion diversity for causal inference without interfering with the distillation loss. On VBench (20s generation), HiAR achieves the best overall score and the lowest temporal drift among all compared methods.

ru.title: Одинаковый уровень шума — секрет стабильной генерации видео
ru.desc: HiAR — это иерархическая авторегрессивная диффузионная модель для генерации видео произвольной длины. Ключевая идея заключается в кондиционировании на контексте с тем же уровнем шума, что и генерируемый блок, что обеспечивает временную согласованность и снижает накопление ошибок предсказания. Модель использует обратный порядок генерации, выполняя причинную генерацию всех блоков на каждом шаге денойзинга, что позволяет распараллелить вычисления и достичь ускорения в 1.8 раза. Для сохранения разнообразия движений применяется forward-KL регуляризация, которая предотвращает схождение модели к движениям с низкой амплитудой.

en.title: HiAR: Enhancing Video Generation with Hierarchical Autoregressive Diffusion
en.desc: The paper introduces HiAR, a hierarchical autoregressive diffusion framework designed to enhance video generation by maintaining temporal continuity and motion diversity. It challenges the conventional approach of conditioning on highly denoised contexts, which can lead to error accumulation and quality degradation. Instead, HiAR conditions on context at the same noise level as the current block, allowing for effective signal transmission while reducing prediction errors. The framework also employs forward-KL regularization to ensure diverse motion during causal inference, resulting in improved performance on video generation tasks.

zh.title: HiAR：提升视频生成的分层自回归框架
zh.desc: HiAR是一种分层自回归扩散框架，旨在改善视频生成的质量。它通过在相同噪声水平下对上下文进行条件处理，保持时间连续性和运动多样性。与传统方法不同，HiAR不需要高度去噪的上下文，而是采用共享噪声水平的上下文来减少错误传播。通过这种方式，HiAR在生成速度和视频质量上都取得了显著的提升。

papers.title: \$OneMillion-Bench: How Far are Language Agents from Human Experts?
papers.abstract: Abstract A new benchmark evaluates language models on complex, real-world professional tasks requiring multi-step reasoning, evidence resolution, and domain-specific decision-making across multiple industries.  					AI-generated summary As language models (LMs) evolve from chat assistants to long-horizon agents capable of multi-step reasoning and tool use, existing benchmarks remain largely confined to structured or exam-style tasks that fall short of real-world professional demands. To this end, we introduce \OneMillion-Bench OneMillion-Bench, a benchmark of 400 expert-curated tasks spanning Law, Finance, Industry, Healthcare, and Natural Science, built to evaluate agents across economically consequential scenarios. Unlike prior work, the benchmark requires retrieving authoritative sources, resolving conflicting evidence, applying domain-specific rules, and making constraint decisions, where correctness depends as much on the reasoning process as the final answer. We adopt a rubric-based evaluation protocol scoring factual accuracy, logical coherence, practical feasibility, and professional compliance, focused on expert-level problems to ensure meaningful differentiation across agents. Together, \$OneMillion-Bench provides a unified testbed for assessing agentic reliability, professional depth, and practical readiness in domain-intensive scenarios.

ru.title: Профессиональный бенчмарк для оценки надежности агентов в сложных реальных сценариях
ru.desc: В работе представлен новый бенчмарк OneMillion-Bench, содержащий 400 экспертно подобранных задач из области права, финансов, промышленности, здравоохранения и естественных наук. Бенчмарк предназначен для оценки языковых моделей и агентов на сложных профессиональных задачах, требующих многошагового рассуждения, анализа конфликтующих источников информации и применения специализированных правил. Оценка осуществляется на основе рубрик, измеряющих фактическую точность, логическую согласованность, практическую осуществимость и профессиональное соответствие ответов. Бенчмарк позволяет объективно оценивать надежность, экспертный уровень и готовность языковых моделей к решению реальных профессиональных задач в различных специализированных областях.

en.title: OneMillion-Bench: Evaluating Language Models for Real-World Professional Tasks
en.desc: This paper introduces OneMillion-Bench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate language models on complex, real-world tasks that require multi-step reasoning and domain-specific decision-making. Unlike traditional benchmarks that focus on structured tasks, OneMillion-Bench includes 400 expert-curated tasks across various industries such as Law, Finance, and Healthcare. The evaluation process emphasizes not only the correctness of the final answers but also the reasoning process, requiring models to retrieve authoritative sources and resolve conflicting evidence. This benchmark aims to assess the reliability and practical readiness of language models in professional settings, ensuring they can handle economically significant scenarios effectively.

zh.title: 评估语言模型的专业能力与推理能力
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一个新的基准测试，旨在评估语言模型在复杂的专业任务中的表现。这些任务需要多步骤推理、证据解决和特定领域的决策能力，涵盖法律、金融、工业、医疗和自然科学等多个行业。与以往的基准不同，这个测试要求模型检索权威来源、解决冲突证据，并应用领域特定规则。通过这种方式，研究者能够更好地评估模型在实际应用中的可靠性和专业深度。

papers.title: NLE: Non-autoregressive LLM-based ASR by Transcript Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract A non-autoregressive speech recognition approach formulates acoustic-to-text conversion as conditional transcript editing using a bidirectional language model editor with latent alignment training and interleaved padding for improved efficiency.  					AI-generated summary While autoregressive (AR) LLM-based ASR systems achieve strong accuracy, their sequential decoding limits parallelism and incurs high latency. We propose NLE, a non-autoregressive (NAR) approach that formulates speech recognition as conditional transcript editing, enabling fully parallel prediction. NLE extracts acoustic embeddings and an initial hypothesis from a pretrained speech encoder, then refines the hypothesis using a bidirectional LLM editor trained with a latent alignment objective. An interleaved padding strategy exploits the identity mapping bias of Transformers, allowing the model to focus on corrections rather than full reconstruction. On the Open ASR leaderboard, NLE++ achieves 5.67% average WER with an RTFx (inverse real-time factor) of 1630. In single-utterance scenarios, NLE achieves 27x speedup over the AR baseline, making it suitable for real-time applications.

ru.title: Параллельное редактирование вместо последовательного: ускоренное распознавание речи в 27 раз
ru.desc: Статья представляет NLE — неавторегрессивный подход к автоматическому распознаванию речи, который формулирует задачу как условное редактирование транскрипции. Вместо последовательного декодирования, как в традиционных LLM-моделях, система параллельно предсказывает все исправления текста, используя двунаправленный языковой редактор. Модель обучается с объективом скрытого выравнивания и использует интерполированное дополнение для эффективного использования предвзятости нейронных сетей Transformer на тождественное отображение. Подход достигает значительного ускорения (27x) в сравнении с авторегрессивным базовым решением, сохраняя высокую точность распознавания при низкой задержке.

en.title: Revolutionizing Speech Recognition with Non-Autoregressive Editing
en.desc: This paper presents a non-autoregressive (NAR) approach to speech recognition that enhances efficiency by treating acoustic-to-text conversion as conditional transcript editing. The method utilizes a bidirectional language model (LLM) editor, which refines initial hypotheses generated from a pretrained speech encoder. By employing latent alignment training and an interleaved padding strategy, the model can focus on making corrections rather than reconstructing the entire output. The results show significant improvements in word error rate (WER) and processing speed, making this approach suitable for real-time applications.

zh.title: 非自回归语音识别：高效且快速的转录编辑
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种非自回归的语音识别方法，将声学到文本的转换视为条件转录编辑。该方法使用双向语言模型编辑器，并通过潜在对齐训练和交错填充策略来提高效率。与传统的自回归系统相比，这种方法能够实现完全并行的预测，从而显著降低延迟。实验结果表明，该方法在语音识别任务中表现出色，适合实时应用。

papers.title: TDM-R1: Reinforcing Few-Step Diffusion Models with Non-Differentiable Reward
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel reinforcement learning approach called TDM-R1 is introduced to enhance few-step generative models by incorporating non-differentiable rewards through surrogate reward learning and generator learning decoupling.  					AI-generated summary While few-step generative models have enabled powerful image and video generation at significantly lower cost, generic reinforcement learning (RL) paradigms for few-step models remain an unsolved problem. Existing RL approaches for few-step diffusion models strongly rely on back-propagating through differentiable reward models, thereby excluding the majority of important real-world reward signals, e.g., non-differentiable rewards such as humans' binary likeness, object counts, etc. To properly incorporate non-differentiable rewards to improve few-step generative models, we introduce TDM-R1, a novel reinforcement learning paradigm built upon a leading few-step model, Trajectory Distribution Matching (TDM). TDM-R1 decouples the learning process into surrogate reward learning and generator learning. Furthermore, we developed practical methods to obtain per-step reward signals along the deterministic generation trajectory of TDM, resulting in a unified RL post-training method that significantly improves few-step models' ability with generic rewards. We conduct extensive experiments ranging from text-rendering, visual quality, and preference alignment. All results demonstrate that TDM-R1 is a powerful reinforcement learning paradigm for few-step text-to-image models, achieving state-of-the-art reinforcement learning performances on both in-domain and out-of-domain metrics. Furthermore, TDM-R1 also scales effectively to the recent strong Z-Image model, consistently outperforming both its 100-NFE and few-step variants with only 4 NFEs. Project page: https://github.com/Luo-Yihong/TDM-R1

Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou)
The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen

ru.title: Обучение с подкреплением для быстрых генеративных моделей через недифференцируемые награды
ru.desc: В статье предложен новый подход TDM-R1 для обучения с подкреплением, который позволяет использовать недифференцируемые функции награды в быстрых генеративных моделях. Ключевая инновация заключается в разделении процесса обучения на два этапа: обучение суррогатной модели награды и обучение генератора. Метод основан на парадигме Trajectory Distribution Matching и позволяет извлекать сигналы награды для каждого шага генерации, включая такие реальные награды как бинарные предпочтения человека и подсчёт объектов. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют значительное улучшение качества text-to-image генерации с минимальным количеством шагов прямых вычислений.

en.title: Revolutionizing Few-Step Generative Models with TDM-R1
en.desc: The paper introduces TDM-R1, a new reinforcement learning method designed to improve few-step generative models by effectively using non-differentiable rewards. Traditional reinforcement learning struggles with these types of rewards, which are crucial for real-world applications, such as human preferences and object counts. TDM-R1 separates the learning into two parts: one for learning surrogate rewards and another for training the generator, allowing for better integration of these complex reward signals. Extensive experiments show that TDM-R1 enhances the performance of few-step text-to-image models, achieving top results in various metrics and demonstrating its scalability to advanced models.

zh.title: TDM-R1：提升少步生成模型的强化学习新方法
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种新颖的强化学习方法TDM-R1，旨在通过代理奖励学习和生成器学习解耦来增强少步生成模型。现有的少步扩散模型强化学习方法依赖于可微分奖励模型，无法有效利用许多重要的现实世界奖励信号。TDM-R1通过将学习过程分为代理奖励学习和生成器学习，成功地将非可微分奖励纳入少步生成模型中。实验结果表明，TDM-R1在文本到图像模型上表现出色，达到了最先进的强化学习性能。

papers.title: Scaling Agentic Capabilities, Not Context: Efficient Reinforcement Finetuning for Large Toolspaces
papers.abstract: Abstract ATLAS enables small language models to effectively operate in large-scale tool environments through reinforcement fine-tuning that learns context control and execution structure, achieving performance comparable to larger models under stricter resource constraints.  					AI-generated summary Agentic systems operating over large tool ecosystems must plan and execute long-horizon workflows under weak or non-verifiable supervision. While frontier models mitigate these challenges through scale and large context budgets, small language models (SLMs) remain brittle: eager tool loading saturates context, execution errors compound over time, and sparse rewards limit learning. We introduce ATLAS, a reinforcement finetuning framework that enables SLMs to operate effectively in large-scale toolspace environments by learning how to acquire context and how to execute actions. Our approach makes two key contributions. First, we treat context control and execution structure as learnable decisions, combining iterative tool loading with programmatic tool orchestration to bound context growth and stabilize long-horizon trajectories. Second, we propose rubric-based reinforcement finetuning, which decomposes task success into structured, task-aligned criteria and enables scalable training using small judge models. Across MCP benchmarks, these design choices yield large and consistent gains over generic RL baselines, allowing a 4B SLM to approach frontier-agent performance under far tighter parameter and context budgets.

ru.title: Малые модели для больших экосистем инструментов
ru.desc: ATLAS — это фреймворк для обучения с подкреплением, который позволяет малым языковым моделям эффективно работать в средах с большим количеством инструментов. Ключевая идея заключается в том, чтобы научить модель самостоятельно контролировать загрузку контекста и структурировать исполнение действий, избегая перегрузки памяти и накопления ошибок. Фреймворк использует обучение на основе рубрик, которое разбивает успех задачи на структурированные критерии и позволяет масштабировать процесс обучения с помощью небольших моделей-судей. Результаты показывают, что 4-миллиардная модель достигает производительности передовых агентных систем при значительно меньшем использовании параметров и контекста.

en.title: Empowering Small Models in Complex Tool Environments with ATLAS
en.desc: The paper introduces ATLAS, a framework that enhances small language models (SLMs) to function effectively in complex tool environments through reinforcement fine-tuning. It focuses on learning how to manage context and execute actions, addressing the limitations of SLMs that struggle with context saturation and execution errors. ATLAS combines iterative tool loading with structured execution to improve decision-making and stabilize long-term performance. The framework also employs rubric-based reinforcement fine-tuning, which breaks down task success into specific criteria, leading to significant performance improvements over traditional reinforcement learning methods.

zh.title: ATLAS：小型模型在大规模工具环境中的新突破
zh.desc: ATLAS是一个强化微调框架，旨在帮助小型语言模型在大规模工具环境中有效运作。它通过学习上下文控制和执行结构，使小型语言模型在资源限制下的表现接近大型模型。该方法的两个关键贡献是将上下文控制和执行结构视为可学习的决策，并提出基于标准的强化微调，以便使用小型评判模型进行可扩展训练。通过这些设计选择，ATLAS在多个基准测试中显著提高了小型语言模型的性能。

papers.title: Concept-Guided Fine-Tuning: Steering ViTs away from Spurious Correlations to Improve Robustness
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel fine-tuning framework for Vision Transformers that improves robustness under distribution shifts by aligning model attention with concept-level semantics using automatically generated, label-free concept masks from large language and vision models.  					AI-generated summary Vision Transformers (ViTs) often degrade under distribution shifts because they rely on spurious correlations, such as background cues, rather than semantically meaningful features. Existing regularization methods, typically relying on simple foreground-background masks, which fail to capture the fine-grained semantic concepts that define an object (e.g., ``long beak'' and ``wings'' for a ``bird''). As a result, these methods provide limited robustness to distribution shifts. To address this limitation, we introduce a novel finetuning framework that steers model reasoning toward concept-level semantics. Our approach optimizes the model's internal relevance maps to align with spatially grounded concept masks. These masks are generated automatically, without manual annotation: class-relevant concepts are first proposed using an LLM-based, label-free method, and then segmented using a VLM. The finetuning objective aligns relevance with these concept regions while simultaneously suppressing focus on spurious background areas. Notably, this process requires only a minimal set of images and uses half of the dataset classes. Extensive experiments on five out-of-distribution benchmarks demonstrate that our method improves robustness across multiple ViT-based models. Furthermore, we show that the resulting relevance maps exhibit stronger alignment with semantic object parts, offering a scalable path toward more robust and interpretable vision models. Finally, we confirm that concept-guided masks provide more effective supervision for model robustness than conventional segmentation maps, supporting our central hypothesis.

ru.title: Семантические концепты вместо шума: путь к робустному зрению
ru.desc: Авторы предлагают новый метод fine-tuning для Vision Transformers, который улучшает устойчивость модели к распределительным сдвигам данных. Подход использует автоматически сгенерированные маски концептов без ручной разметки, полученные с помощью больших языковых и мультимодальных моделей. Метод оптимизирует внутренние карты релевантности модели для выравнивания с семантически значимыми концептами объектов (например, длинный клюв и крылья для птицы), подавляя при этом внимание к фоновым артефактам. Экспериментальные результаты на пяти бенчмарках out-of-distribution демонстрируют значительное улучшение робустности и интерпретируемости Vision Transformers.

en.title: Enhancing Vision Transformers with Concept-Level Semantics
en.desc: This paper presents a new fine-tuning framework for Vision Transformers (ViTs) that enhances their robustness when faced with distribution shifts. The framework aligns the model's attention with meaningful semantic concepts by using automatically generated concept masks, which are created without manual labeling. Unlike traditional methods that rely on simple foreground-background masks, this approach captures detailed object features, improving the model's ability to focus on relevant aspects while ignoring irrelevant background cues. The results show that this method significantly boosts the performance of ViTs across various benchmarks, leading to more interpretable and resilient vision models.

zh.title: 概念引导的微调框架提升视觉模型鲁棒性
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的微调框架，旨在提高视觉变换器（ViTs）在分布变化下的鲁棒性。该框架通过自动生成的无标签概念掩码，将模型的注意力与概念级语义对齐，从而改善模型的推理能力。与传统的前景-背景掩码不同，我们的方法能够捕捉定义对象的细粒度语义概念。实验结果表明，该方法在多个ViT模型上显著提高了鲁棒性，并且生成的相关性图与语义对象部分的对齐程度更强。

papers.title: Unlocking Data Value in Finance: A Study on Distillation and Difficulty-Aware Training
papers.abstract: Abstract High-quality dataset curation and specialized training methods improve financial language model performance through enhanced reasoning and reduced factual errors.  					AI-generated summary Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong general capabilities, yet their deployment in finance remains challenging due to dense domain-specific terminology, stringent numerical reasoning requirements, and low tolerance for factual errors. We conduct a controlled empirical study showing that in specialized vertical domains, performance is largely determined by the quality and difficulty/verifiability profile of post-training data. We introduce ODA-Fin-SFT-318k, constructed via multi-stage distillation and verification to produce high-quality Chain-of-Thought supervision, and ODA-Fin-RL-12k, curated for hard-but-verifiable tasks that balance reward precision and task diversity. Using standard SFT and RL pipelines, we show that high-quality CoT distillation establishes a robust foundation during SFT, while difficulty- and verifiability-aware sampling improves RL generalization. Evaluated on nine benchmarks spanning general financial tasks, sentiment analysis, and numerical reasoning, our ODA-Fin-RL-8B consistently surpasses open-source state-of-the-art (SOTA) financial LLMs of comparable size. We release our ODA-Fin-SFT-318k and ODA-Fin-RL-12k datasets, along with trained models to advance data-centric financial AI research.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Качество данных — ключ к финансовой компетентности языковых моделей
ru.desc: В работе показано, что качество данных для обучения является ключевым фактором для повышения производительности больших языковых моделей в финансовой сфере. Авторы создали два специализированных датасета: ODA-Fin-SFT-318k с высококачественными цепочками рассуждений, полученными через многоэтапную дистилляцию, и ODA-Fin-RL-12k с тщательно отобранными сложными задачами для обучения с подкреплением. Они применили стандартные методы fine-tuning (SFT) и обучения с подкреплением (RL), демонстрируя, что качественная дистилляция цепочек мыслей создает прочную основу, а выборка с учетом сложности улучшает обобщение модели. Полученная модель ODA-Fin-RL-8B превосходит существующие открытые финансовые LLM сравнимого размера на девяти бенчмарках, включая задачи анализа тональности и численного рассуждения.

en.title: Enhancing Financial AI with Quality Data and Training
en.desc: This paper discusses how improving the quality of datasets and training methods can enhance the performance of financial language models. It highlights the challenges faced by AI in finance, such as complex terminology and the need for accurate numerical reasoning. The authors introduce two new datasets, ODA-Fin-SFT-318k and ODA-Fin-RL-12k, which are designed to provide high-quality supervision and support for difficult tasks. Their findings show that using these specialized datasets leads to better performance in various financial benchmarks compared to existing models.

zh.title: 提升金融语言模型性能的关键在于高质量数据与专业训练
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了高质量数据集的整理和专业训练方法如何提升金融语言模型的表现。研究表明，在特定领域中，模型的性能主要取决于后期训练数据的质量和难度。我们介绍了两个新数据集，ODA-Fin-SFT-318k和ODA-Fin-RL-12k，旨在提供高质量的推理监督和适合验证的任务。通过标准的监督学习和强化学习流程，我们的模型在多个金融任务基准测试中表现优异，超越了同类开源模型。

papers.title: Training-free Latent Inter-Frame Pruning with Attention Recovery
papers.abstract: Abstract Latent Inter-frame Pruning with Attention Recovery framework reduces video generation latency by identifying and skipping redundant latent patches while maintaining quality through attention approximation.  					AI-generated summary Current video generation models suffer from high computational latency, making real-time applications prohibitively costly. In this paper, we address this limitation by exploiting the temporal redundancy inherent in video latent patches. To this end, we propose the Latent Inter-frame Pruning with Attention Recovery (LIPAR) framework, which detects and skips recomputing duplicated latent patches. Additionally, we introduce a novel Attention Recovery mechanism that approximates the attention values of pruned tokens, thereby removing visual artifacts arising from naively applying the pruning method. Empirically, our method increases video editing throughput by 1.45times, on average achieving 12.2 FPS on an NVIDIA A6000 compared to the baseline 8.4 FPS. The proposed method does not compromise generation quality and can be seamlessly integrated with the model without additional training. Our approach effectively bridges the gap between traditional compression algorithms and modern generative pipelines.
affiliations: Department of CS, University of Texas at Austin
Department of ECE, University of Texas at Austin

ru.title: Ускорение видеогенерации через удаление временной избыточности с восстановлением внимания
ru.desc: В статье предложен фреймворк LIPAR для ускорения генерации видео путём выявления и пропуска вычисления избыточных скрытых патчей между кадрами. Авторы вводят механизм восстановления внимания, который аппроксимирует значения внимания для удалённых токенов и предотвращает появление визуальных артефактов. Метод увеличивает пропускную способность видеоредактирования в 1.45 раза, достигая 12.2 FPS на GPU NVIDIA A6000 без снижения качества генерации. Подход можно интегрировать в существующие модели без дополнительного обучения.

en.title: Boosting Video Generation Speed with Smart Pruning!
en.desc: This paper presents the Latent Inter-frame Pruning with Attention Recovery (LIPAR) framework, which aims to reduce the latency in video generation by identifying and skipping redundant latent patches. By leveraging the temporal redundancy in video data, LIPAR avoids unnecessary computations, thus improving efficiency. The framework includes an Attention Recovery mechanism that ensures the quality of generated videos remains high by approximating attention values for pruned tokens. As a result, LIPAR significantly enhances video editing throughput while maintaining visual fidelity, making it suitable for real-time applications.

zh.title: 提升视频生成效率的创新方法
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为潜在帧间修剪与注意力恢复框架（LIPAR），旨在减少视频生成的延迟。该方法通过识别和跳过冗余的潜在补丁，利用视频中的时间冗余来提高生成效率。我们还引入了一种新的注意力恢复机制，能够近似修剪后标记的注意力值，从而消除视觉伪影。实验结果表明，该方法在不降低生成质量的情况下，视频编辑的吞吐量提高了1.45倍。

papers.title: Agentic Critical Training
papers.abstract: Abstract Agentic Critical Training (ACT) is a reinforcement learning approach that trains language model agents to autonomously reason about action quality by directly rewarding correct judgment between alternatives, outperforming imitation learning and existing reflection injection methods.  					AI-generated summary Training large language models (LLMs) as autonomous agents often begins with imitation learning, but it only teaches agents what to do without understanding why: agents never contrast successful actions against suboptimal alternatives and thus lack awareness of action quality. Recent approaches attempt to address this by introducing self-reflection supervision derived from contrasts between expert and alternative actions. However, the training paradigm fundamentally remains imitation learning: the model imitates pre-constructed reflection text rather than learning to reason autonomously. We propose Agentic Critical Training (ACT), a reinforcement learning paradigm that trains agents to identify the better action among alternatives. By rewarding whether the model's judgment is correct, ACT drives the model to autonomously develop reasoning about action quality, producing genuine self-reflection rather than imitating it. Across three challenging agent benchmarks, ACT consistently improves agent performance when combined with different post-training methods. It achieves an average improvement of 5.07 points over imitation learning and 4.62 points over reinforcement learning. Compared to approaches that inject reflection capability through knowledge distillation, ACT also demonstrates clear advantages, yielding an average improvement of 2.42 points. Moreover, ACT enables strong out-of-distribution generalization on agentic benchmarks and improves performance on general reasoning benchmarks without any reasoning-specific training data, highlighting the value of our method. These results suggest that ACT is a promising path toward developing more reflective and capable LLM agents.

ru.title: Обучение агентов истинной саморефлексии через подкрепление за правильное суждение
ru.desc: В статье предлагается метод Agentic Critical Training (ACT) — подход обучения с подкреплением, который обучает языковые модели-агенты автономно оценивать качество действий путём прямого вознаграждения за правильное суждение между альтернативами. В отличие от имитационного обучения, которое только показывает агентам что делать, ACT учит их понимать почему через контрастивное сравнение успешных и неудачных действий. Метод вознаграждает корректность рассуждений модели о качестве действий, что позволяет агентам развивать подлинную саморефлексию вместо её имитации. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют улучшение производительности на 5.07 пункта над имитационным обучением и хорошую обобщающую способность на распределениях данных, отличных от тренировочных.

en.title: Empowering Agents with Autonomous Reasoning through ACT
en.desc: Agentic Critical Training (ACT) is a novel reinforcement learning method designed to enhance the reasoning capabilities of language model agents. Unlike traditional imitation learning, which only teaches agents to mimic actions without understanding their quality, ACT rewards agents for correctly judging the better action among alternatives. This approach fosters genuine self-reflection and autonomous reasoning, leading to improved performance across various benchmarks. The results indicate that ACT not only surpasses imitation learning but also enhances generalization and reasoning abilities in language models without requiring additional training data.

zh.title: 自主推理，提升行动质量的训练方法
zh.desc: Agentic Critical Training (ACT) 是一种强化学习方法，旨在训练语言模型代理自主判断行动质量。与模仿学习不同，ACT 通过直接奖励正确的判断来提高代理的推理能力，使其能够识别更优的行动。该方法在多个代理基准测试中表现出色，平均提高了 5.07 分，相较于现有的反思注入方法也有明显优势。ACT 的成功表明，它为开发更具反思能力和更强大的语言模型代理提供了新的方向。

papers.title: PIRA-Bench: A Transition from Reactive GUI Agents to GUI-based Proactive Intent Recommendation Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract PIRA-Bench presents a benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models on proactive GUI agent tasks using continuous visual inputs, while PIRF offers a memory-aware framework for handling complex, noisy screen trajectories.  					AI-generated summary Current Graphical User Interface (GUI) agents operate primarily under a reactive paradigm: a user must provide an explicit instruction for the agent to execute a task. However, an intelligent AI assistant should be proactive, which is capable of anticipating user intentions directly from continuous visual inputs, such as mobile or desktop screenshots, and offering timely recommendations without explicit user prompting. Transitioning to this proactive paradigm presents significant challenges. Real-world screen activity is rarely linear; it consists of long-horizon trajectories fraught with noisy browsing, meaningless actions, and multithreaded task-switching. To address this gap, we introduce PIRA-Bench (Proactive Intent Recommendation Agent Benchmark), a novel benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models (MLLMs) on continuous, weakly-supervised visual inputs. Unlike reactive datasets, PIRA-Bench features complex trajectories with multiple interleaved intents and noisy segments with various user profile contexts, challenging agents to detect actionable events while fitting to user preferences. Furthermore, we propose the PIRF baseline, a memory-aware, state-tracking framework that empowers general MLLMs to manage multiple task threads and handle misleading visual inputs. PIRA-Bench serves as an initial step toward robust and proactive GUI-based personal assistants.

ru.title: Проактивные GUI-ассистенты, предугадывающие намерения пользователя
ru.desc: В статье представлена PIRA-Bench, бенчмарк для оценки мультимодальных больших языковых моделей на задачах проактивного GUI-агента с непрерывными визуальными входными данными. Авторы предлагают PIRF — фреймворк с механизмом памяти, способный обрабатывать сложные и шумные траектории взаимодействий пользователя с экраном, включая многопоточное переключение между задачами. В отличие от реактивных подходов, где агент ждёт явной команды, предлагаемый подход позволяет модели предвидеть намерения пользователя прямо из последовательности скриншотов и давать своевременные рекомендации. Работа направлена на создание более интеллектуальных персональных ассистентов на основе GUI, способных понимать контекст и избегать влияния шумовых данных.

en.title: Empowering Proactive AI Assistants with PIRA-Bench
en.desc: PIRA-Bench is a new benchmark designed to evaluate multimodal large language models (MLLMs) on proactive tasks involving graphical user interfaces (GUIs). Unlike traditional reactive systems that wait for user commands, this benchmark encourages models to anticipate user needs from continuous visual inputs like screenshots. The challenge lies in managing complex, noisy screen activities that include multiple tasks and distractions. To tackle this, the paper introduces the PIRF framework, which helps MLLMs track state and manage various tasks effectively, paving the way for smarter AI assistants.

zh.title: 主动智能助手的未来
zh.desc: PIRA-Bench是一个用于评估多模态大型语言模型在主动图形用户界面（GUI）代理任务中的基准测试。该基准测试利用连续的视觉输入，旨在使AI助手能够主动预测用户意图，而不是仅仅响应用户的指令。为了应对复杂和嘈杂的屏幕轨迹，PIRF框架被提出，以帮助模型管理多线程任务并处理误导性的视觉输入。通过这些创新，PIRA-Bench为开发更智能的个人助手奠定了基础。

papers.title: AutoResearch-RL: Perpetual Self-Evaluating Reinforcement Learning Agents for Autonomous Neural Architecture Discovery
papers.abstract: Abstract An autonomous reinforcement learning framework conducts continuous neural architecture and hyperparameter research without human intervention, achieving performance comparable to hand-tuned baselines through automated experimentation.  					AI-generated summary We present AutoResearch-RL, a framework in which a reinforcement learning agent conducts open-ended neural architecture and hyperparameter research without human supervision, running perpetually until a termination oracle signals convergence or resource exhaustion. At each step the agent proposes a code modification to a target training script, executes it under a fixed wall clock time budget, observes a scalar reward derived from validation bits-per-byte (val-bpb), and updates its policy via Proximal Policy Optimisation (PPO).   The key design insight is the separation of three concerns: (i) a frozen environment (data pipeline, evaluation protocol, and constants) that guarantees fair cross-experiment comparison; (ii) a mutable target file (train.py) that represents the agent's editable state; and (iii) a meta-learner (the RL agent itself) that accumulates a growing trajectory of experiment outcomes and uses them to inform subsequent proposals.   We formalise this as a Markov Decision Process, derive convergence guarantees under mild assumptions, and demonstrate empirically on a single GPU nanochat pretraining benchmark that AutoResearch-RL discovers configurations that match or exceed hand-tuned baselines after approximately 300 overnight iterations, with no human in the loop.

ru.title: Автоматический поиск архитектур нейросетей через обучение с подкреплением
ru.desc: В статье представлена система AutoResearch-RL, которая использует агент обучения с подкреплением для автоматического поиска оптимальной архитектуры нейронной сети и гиперпараметров без участия человека. Агент итеративно предлагает модификации к коду обучения, выполняет эксперименты в рамках временного бюджета и получает вознаграждение на основе валидационной метрики, обновляя свою политику методом PPO. Ключевой идеей является разделение трёх компонентов: замороженной окружающей среды для справедливого сравнения, редактируемого файла обучения и мета-обучающегося агента, накапливающего историю экспериментов. Система демонстрирует, что за примерно 300 итераций ночного обучения может найти конфигурации, сравнимые или превосходящие ручную настройку специалистами.

en.title: Autonomous Neural Architecture Search with Reinforcement Learning
en.desc: This paper introduces AutoResearch-RL, a framework that uses reinforcement learning to autonomously explore neural architecture and hyperparameter optimization without human input. The framework operates continuously, making code modifications to a training script and evaluating performance based on a scalar reward derived from validation metrics. It separates the environment, the target file for modifications, and the meta-learner to ensure fair comparisons and effective learning. The results show that AutoResearch-RL can achieve performance on par with or better than manually tuned models after a relatively short period of experimentation.

zh.title: 自主强化学习：无人工干预的自动研究
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种自主强化学习框架，名为AutoResearch-RL，能够在没有人工干预的情况下进行神经网络架构和超参数的研究。该框架通过自动实验，达到与手动调优基准相当的性能。它的设计理念是将环境、可变目标文件和元学习者分开，以确保实验的公平性和有效性。实验结果表明，AutoResearch-RL在约300次迭代后，能够发现与手动调优相匹配或更优的配置。

papers.title: PureCC: Pure Learning for Text-to-Image Concept Customization
papers.abstract: Abstract PureCC presents a concept customization method that preserves original model behavior through decoupled learning and adaptive guidance scaling.  					AI-generated summary Existing concept customization methods have achieved remarkable outcomes in high-fidelity and multi-concept customization. However, they often neglect the influence on the original model's behavior and capabilities when learning new personalized concepts. To address this issue, we propose PureCC. PureCC introduces a novel decoupled learning objective for concept customization, which combines the implicit guidance of the target concept with the original conditional prediction. This separated form enables PureCC to substantially focus on the original model during training. Moreover, based on this objective, PureCC designs a dual-branch training pipeline that includes a frozen extractor providing purified target concept representations as implicit guidance and a trainable flow model producing the original conditional prediction, jointly achieving pure learning for personalized concepts. Furthermore, PureCC introduces a novel adaptive guidance scale λ^star to dynamically adjust the guidance strength of the target concept, balancing customization fidelity and model preservation. Extensive experiments show that PureCC achieves state-of-the-art performance in preserving the original behavior and capabilities while enabling high-fidelity concept customization. The code is available at https://github.com/lzc-sg/PureCC.
affiliations: Guangdong Provincial Key Laboratory of Intelligent Information Processing, Shenzhen University
School of Computer Science & Software Engineering, Shenzhen University

ru.title: Сохранение оригинальности при персонализации моделей
ru.desc: PureCC предлагает новый метод кастомизации концепций для моделей генеративного AI, который сохраняет исходное поведение модели через разделённую обучающую задачу. Метод использует двухветвевую архитектуру: замороженный экстрактор генерирует чистые представления целевого концепта, а обучаемая flow-модель воспроизводит исходное условное предсказание. PureCC вводит адаптивный коэффициент масштабирования λ*, который динамически балансирует точность кастомизации и сохранение способностей исходной модели. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что метод достигает лучших показателей как в качественной кастомизации новых концептов, так и в сохранении исходной производительности модели.

en.title: Preserve Original Model Behavior with PureCC Customization
en.desc: PureCC is a method for customizing machine learning models while keeping their original behavior intact. It uses a technique called decoupled learning, which separates the learning of new concepts from the model's existing capabilities. This approach includes a dual-branch training pipeline that helps the model learn new concepts without losing its original skills. Additionally, PureCC employs an adaptive guidance scale to fine-tune how much influence the new concepts have, ensuring high-quality customization without compromising the model's performance.

zh.title: PureCC：个性化概念定制的新方法
zh.desc: PureCC是一种概念定制方法，通过解耦学习和自适应引导缩放来保持原始模型的行为。它引入了一种新的解耦学习目标，将目标概念的隐式引导与原始条件预测相结合。PureCC设计了一个双分支训练管道，其中包括一个冻结的提取器和一个可训练的流模型，确保在个性化概念学习中专注于原始模型。通过引入自适应引导尺度λ^star，PureCC能够动态调整目标概念的引导强度，实现高保真度的定制和模型的保留。

papers.title: From Narrow to Panoramic Vision: Attention-Guided Cold-Start Reshapes Multimodal Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract The study introduces a novel attention-based metric called Visual Attention Score to analyze cold-start initialization in multimodal large reasoning models, identifying a counter-intuitive phenomenon termed Lazy Attention Localization and proposing a framework called Attention-Guided Visual Anchoring and Reflection to improve multimodal reasoning performance.  					AI-generated summary The cold-start initialization stage plays a pivotal role in training Multimodal Large Reasoning Models (MLRMs), yet its mechanisms remain insufficiently understood. To analyze this stage, we introduce the Visual Attention Score (VAS), an attention-based metric that quantifies how much a model attends to visual tokens. We find that reasoning performance is strongly correlated with VAS (r=0.9616): models with higher VAS achieve substantially stronger multimodal reasoning. Surprisingly, multimodal cold-start fails to elevate VAS, resulting in attention distributions close to the base model, whereas text-only cold-start leads to a clear increase. We term this counter-intuitive phenomenon Lazy Attention Localization. To validate its causal role, we design training-free interventions that directly modulate attention allocation during inference, performance gains of 1-2% without any retraining. Building on these insights, we further propose Attention-Guided Visual Anchoring and Reflection (AVAR), a comprehensive cold-start framework that integrates visual-anchored data synthesis, attention-guided objectives, and visual-anchored reward shaping. Applied to Qwen2.5-VL-7B, AVAR achieves an average gain of 7.0% across 7 multimodal reasoning benchmarks. Ablation studies further confirm that each component of AVAR contributes step-wise to the overall gains. The code, data, and models are available at https://github.com/lrlbbzl/Qwen-AVAR.

ru.title: Направленное внимание к визуальной информации для инициализации мультимодальных моделей рассуждения
ru.desc: В исследовании предложена новая метрика Visual Attention Score для анализа инициализации мультимодальных больших моделей рассуждения, которая показывает, насколько модель концентрируется на визуальных токенах. Авторы обнаружили парадоксальное явление под названием Lazy Attention Localization, при котором мультимодальная инициализация не улучшает внимание модели к визуальной информации, в отличие от текстовой инициализации. Они разработали фреймворк AVAR, который комбинирует синтез данных с визуальными якорями, управляемые вниманием целевые функции и формирование наград, ориентированное на визуальные якоря. При применении к модели Qwen2.5-VL-7B AVAR достигает среднего улучшения на 7.0% по семи мультимодальным бенчмаркам оценки.

en.title: Enhancing Multimodal Reasoning with Visual Attention Metrics
en.desc: This paper presents a new metric called Visual Attention Score (VAS) to evaluate how well multimodal large reasoning models (MLRMs) utilize visual information during cold-start initialization. The authors discover a surprising effect known as Lazy Attention Localization, where models do not improve their attention to visual tokens as expected during cold-start with multimodal data. To address this issue, they propose a framework called Attention-Guided Visual Anchoring and Reflection (AVAR), which enhances reasoning performance by integrating visual data synthesis and attention-guided objectives. The results show that AVAR significantly boosts performance across various benchmarks, demonstrating the importance of effective attention allocation in MLRMs.

zh.title: 提升多模态推理的新方法
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种新的基于注意力的指标，称为视觉注意力评分（VAS），用于分析多模态大推理模型中的冷启动初始化。我们发现，推理性能与VAS之间存在强相关性，VAS较高的模型在多模态推理中表现更强。令人惊讶的是，多模态冷启动未能提高VAS，导致注意力分布接近基础模型，而仅文本的冷启动则明显提高了VAS。为了解决这一问题，我们提出了一个名为注意力引导的视觉锚定与反思（AVAR）的框架，以提升多模态推理性能。

papers.title: Scale Space Diffusion
papers.abstract: Abstract Scale-space theory connects diffusion models' information hierarchy to low-pass filtering, leading to a framework that combines scale spaces with diffusion processes for efficient image processing.  					AI-generated summary Diffusion models degrade images through noise, and reversing this process reveals an information hierarchy across timesteps. Scale-space theory exhibits a similar hierarchy via low-pass filtering. We formalize this connection and show that highly noisy diffusion states contain no more information than small, downsampled images - raising the question of why they must be processed at full resolution. To address this, we fuse scale spaces into the diffusion process by formulating a family of diffusion models with generalized linear degradations and practical implementations. Using downsampling as the degradation yields our proposed Scale Space Diffusion. To support Scale Space Diffusion, we introduce Flexi-UNet, a UNet variant that performs resolution-preserving and resolution-increasing denoising using only the necessary parts of the network. We evaluate our framework on CelebA and ImageNet and analyze its scaling behavior across resolutions and network depths. Our project website ( https://prateksha.github.io/projects/scale-space-diffusion/ ) is available publicly.

ru.title: Масштабные пространства в диффузионных моделях для эффективной обработки изображений
ru.desc: Статья связывает теорию масштабного пространства с диффузионными моделями, показывая, что сильно зашумлённые состояния содержат не больше информации, чем маленькие уменьшенные изображения. Авторы предлагают Scale Space Diffusion — семейство диффузионных моделей с обобщенными линейными деградациями, которые позволяют эффективнее обрабатывать изображения. Для поддержки этого подхода разработан Flexi-UNet, вариант архитектуры UNet, который выполняет денойзинг при сохранении и увеличении разрешения, используя только необходимые части сети. Экспериментальная оценка на датасетах CelebA и ImageNet демонстрирует эффективность масштабирования метода.

en.title: Enhancing Image Processing with Scale Space Diffusion
en.desc: This paper connects scale-space theory with diffusion models to improve image processing efficiency. It shows that highly noisy images from diffusion processes do not provide more information than smaller, downsampled images. By integrating scale spaces into diffusion processes, the authors propose a new method called Scale Space Diffusion. They also introduce Flexi-UNet, a modified UNet that optimizes denoising while maintaining necessary resolutions, and evaluate their approach on popular datasets like CelebA and ImageNet.

zh.title: 结合尺度空间与扩散模型的高效图像处理
zh.desc: 本论文将尺度空间理论与扩散模型的信息层次联系起来，提出了一种结合尺度空间和扩散过程的高效图像处理框架。扩散模型通过噪声降解图像，逆向过程揭示了时间步长之间的信息层次。我们提出的尺度空间扩散方法通过将下采样作为降解，优化了扩散过程。为支持这一方法，我们引入了Flexi-UNet，这是一种能够在保持分辨率和提高分辨率的去噪网络变体。

papers.title: FVG-PT: Adaptive Foreground View-Guided Prompt Tuning for Vision-Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Foreground attention shifts during CLIP-based prompt tuning are addressed through an adaptive module that enhances foreground view quality and mitigates generalization degradation.  					AI-generated summary CLIP-based prompt tuning enables pretrained Vision-Language Models (VLMs) to efficiently adapt to downstream tasks. Although existing studies have made significant progress, they pay limited attention to changes in the internal attention representations of VLMs during the tuning process. In this paper, we attribute the failure modes of prompt tuning predictions to shifts in foreground attention of the visual encoder, and propose Foreground View-Guided Prompt Tuning (FVG-PT), an adaptive plug-and-play foreground attention guidance module, to alleviate the shifts. Concretely, FVG-PT introduces a learnable Foreground Reliability Gate to automatically enhance the foreground view quality, applies a Foreground Distillation Compensation module to guide visual attention toward the foreground, and further introduces a Prior Calibration module to mitigate generalization degradation caused by excessive focus on the foreground. Experiments on multiple backbone models and datasets show the effectiveness and compatibility of FVG-PT. Codes are available at: https://github.com/JREion/FVG-PT
affiliations: University of Technology Sydney, NSW 2007, Australia

ru.title: Управление вниманием переднего плана для улучшения адаптации визуально-языковых моделей
ru.desc: В статье рассматривается проблема смещения внимания на передний план при настройке промптов в моделях CLIP. Авторы предлагают адаптивный модуль FVG-PT, который включает обучаемый вентиль надежности переднего плана, модуль компенсации дистилляции и модуль калибровки, чтобы направить внимание визуального кодировщика на важные объекты. Решение позволяет повысить качество представления переднего плана и предотвратить деградацию обобщающей способности модели при избыточной фокусировке на переднем плане. Экспериментальные результаты на различных архитектурах и датасетах подтверждают эффективность предложенного подхода.

en.title: Enhancing Foreground Attention in Vision-Language Models
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenges of foreground attention shifts in CLIP-based prompt tuning for Vision-Language Models (VLMs). It introduces a new method called Foreground View-Guided Prompt Tuning (FVG-PT), which includes an adaptive module to improve the quality of foreground views. The method features a Foreground Reliability Gate to enhance attention on important visual elements and a Foreground Distillation Compensation module to maintain focus on the foreground. Additionally, a Prior Calibration module is included to prevent generalization issues that arise from overemphasizing foreground details, demonstrating effectiveness across various models and datasets.

zh.title: 提升前景注意力，优化视觉语言模型
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为前景视图引导提示调优（FVG-PT）的自适应模块，旨在改善基于CLIP的提示调优过程中前景注意力的变化。该模块通过引入可学习的前景可靠性门，自动增强前景视图的质量，并使用前景蒸馏补偿模块引导视觉注意力集中于前景。为了减轻过度关注前景导致的泛化退化，FVG-PT还引入了先验校准模块。实验结果表明，FVG-PT在多个基础模型和数据集上表现出良好的有效性和兼容性。

papers.title: CaTok: Taming Mean Flows for One-Dimensional Causal Image Tokenization
papers.abstract: Abstract CaTok presents a 1D causal image tokenizer with a MeanFlow decoder that enables fast one-step generation and high-fidelity multi-step sampling while achieving state-of-the-art image reconstruction performance.  					AI-generated summary Autoregressive (AR) language models rely on causal tokenization, but extending this paradigm to vision remains non-trivial. Current visual tokenizers either flatten 2D patches into non-causal sequences or enforce heuristic orderings that misalign with the "next-token prediction" pattern. Recent diffusion autoencoders similarly fall short: conditioning the decoder on all tokens lacks causality, while applying nested dropout mechanism introduces imbalance. To address these challenges, we present CaTok, a 1D causal image tokenizer with a MeanFlow decoder. By selecting tokens over time intervals and binding them to the MeanFlow objective, as illustrated in Fig. 1, CaTok learns causal 1D representations that support both fast one-step generation and high-fidelity multi-step sampling, while naturally capturing diverse visual concepts across token intervals. To further stabilize and accelerate training, we propose a straightforward regularization REPA-A, which aligns encoder features with Vision Foundation Models (VFMs). Experiments demonstrate that CaTok achieves state-of-the-art results on ImageNet reconstruction, reaching 0.75 FID, 22.53 PSNR and 0.674 SSIM with fewer training epochs, and the AR model attains performance comparable to leading approaches.
affiliations: Institute of Trustworthy Embodied AI, Fudan University
Shanghai Key Laboratory of Multimodal Embodied AI

ru.title: Каузальная токенизация для одношагового и многошагового синтеза изображений
ru.desc: CaTok представляет новый подход к токенизации изображений, используя одномерное каузальное представление с декодером MeanFlow. Метод решает проблему расширения авторегрессивной парадигмы на компьютерное зрение, где традиционные визуальные токенизаторы либо нарушают причинно-следственные связи, либо используют эвристические упорядочения.
