Предложенный токенизер поддерживает как быструю одношаговую генерацию, так и высокочастотное многошаговое семплирование, обучаясь каузальным представлениям, которые естественно захватывают разнообразные визуальные концепции. Дополнительная регуляризация REPA-A выравнивает признаки энкодера с фундаментальными визуальными моделями, достигая передовых результатов на ImageNet с FID 0.75 и сравнимой производительностью авторегрессивных моделей.

en.title: Causal Tokenization for High-Fidelity Image Generation
en.desc: CaTok introduces a novel 1D causal image tokenizer that enhances image generation and reconstruction tasks. It utilizes a MeanFlow decoder to facilitate rapid one-step generation and high-quality multi-step sampling, achieving impressive results in image fidelity. The method overcomes limitations of existing visual tokenizers by maintaining a causal structure, allowing for effective next-token prediction in visual data. Additionally, the proposed regularization technique, REPA-A, improves training stability and aligns features with Vision Foundation Models, leading to state-of-the-art performance on benchmarks like ImageNet.

zh.title: CaTok：高效的一维因果图像生成
zh.desc: CaTok是一种一维因果图像标记器，配备MeanFlow解码器，能够实现快速的一步生成和高保真的多步采样，同时在图像重建性能上达到最先进水平。该方法通过在时间间隔内选择标记并将其绑定到MeanFlow目标，学习因果的一维表示，支持多样化的视觉概念捕捉。CaTok解决了当前视觉标记器在因果性和顺序对齐方面的挑战，提供了一种新的图像生成方式。实验结果表明，CaTok在ImageNet重建上取得了0.75 FID、22.53 PSNR和0.674 SSIM的优异成绩，且训练周期更少。

papers.title: NaviDriveVLM: Decoupling High-Level Reasoning and Motion Planning for Autonomous Driving
papers.abstract: Abstract NaviDriveVLM presents a decoupled vision-language model framework for autonomous driving that separates high-level reasoning from motion planning, achieving superior performance in end-to-end driving while reducing training costs.  					AI-generated summary Vision-language models (VLMs) have emerged as a promising direction for end-to-end autonomous driving (AD) by jointly modeling visual observations, driving context, and language-based reasoning. However, existing VLM-based systems face a trade-off between high-level reasoning and motion planning: large models offer strong semantic understanding but are costly to adapt for precise control, whereas small VLM models can be fine-tuned efficiently but often exhibit weaker reasoning. We propose NaviDriveVLM, a decoupled framework that separates reasoning from action generation using a large-scale Navigator and a lightweight trainable Driver. This design preserves reasoning ability, reduces training cost, and provides an explicit interpretable intermediate representation for downstream planning. Experiments on the nuScenes benchmark show that NaviDriveVLM outperforms large VLM baselines in end-to-end motion planning.

ru.title: Разделение рассуждений и действий в видео-языковых моделях для автономного вождения
ru.desc: NaviDriveVLM представляет собой модель зрения и языка, которая разделяет процесс принятия решений при автономном вождении на два этапа: высокоуровневое рассуждение и непосредственное планирование движения. Система состоит из большого Навигатора, отвечающего за понимание окружающей обстановки и контекста, и легкого обучаемого Водителя, который преобразует решения в управляющие команды. Такая архитектура позволяет сохранить способность к семантическому анализу сцены, значительно снизить вычислительные затраты на обучение и обеспечить интерпретируемое промежуточное представление для планирования траектории. Экспериментальные результаты на бенчмарке nuScenes демонстрируют превосходство NaviDriveVLM над крупными базовыми моделями в задаче сквозного планирования движения.

en.title: Decoupling Reasoning and Action for Smarter Driving
en.desc: NaviDriveVLM introduces a new framework for autonomous driving that separates high-level reasoning from motion planning. This decoupled approach allows for a large-scale Navigator to handle complex semantic understanding while a lightweight Driver focuses on precise control. By doing this, the model achieves better performance in end-to-end driving tasks without incurring high training costs. Experiments demonstrate that NaviDriveVLM surpasses existing large vision-language models in motion planning effectiveness.

zh.title: 解耦视觉-语言模型，提升自动驾驶性能
zh.desc: NaviDriveVLM提出了一种解耦的视觉-语言模型框架，专为自动驾驶设计。该框架将高层推理与运动规划分开，从而在端到端驾驶中实现了更优的性能，并降低了训练成本。通过使用大规模的导航器和轻量级的可训练驱动器，NaviDriveVLM保持了推理能力，同时提供了明确的中间表示，便于后续规划。实验结果表明，NaviDriveVLM在nuScenes基准测试中超越了大型视觉-语言模型的表现。

papers.title: Building AI Coding Agents for the Terminal: Scaffolding, Harness, Context Engineering, and Lessons Learned
papers.abstract: Abstract OPENDEV is an open-source command-line coding agent that provides autonomous software engineering assistance through specialized model routing, dual-agent architecture, and efficient context management.  					AI-generated summary The landscape of AI coding assistance is undergoing a fundamental shift from complex IDE plugins to versatile, terminal-native agents. Operating directly where developers manage source control, execute builds, and deploy environments, CLI-based agents offer unprecedented autonomy for long-horizon development tasks. In this paper, we present OPENDEV, an open-source, command-line coding agent engineered specifically for this new paradigm. Effective autonomous assistance requires strict safety controls and highly efficient context management to prevent context bloat and reasoning degradation. OPENDEV overcomes these challenges through a compound AI system architecture with workload-specialized model routing, a dual-agent architecture separating planning from execution, lazy tool discovery, and adaptive context compaction that progressively reduces older observations. Furthermore, it employs an automated memory system to accumulate project-specific knowledge across sessions and counteracts instruction fade-out through event-driven system reminders. By enforcing explicit reasoning phases and prioritizing context efficiency, OPENDEV provides a secure, extensible foundation for terminal-first AI assistance, offering a blueprint for robust autonomous software engineering.

ru.title: Автономный разработчик в терминале: эффективная маршрутизация и управление контекстом
ru.desc: OPENDEV — это открытый инструмент командной строки, который обеспечивает автономную помощь в разработке программного обеспечения через специализированную маршрутизацию моделей и двухагентную архитектуру. Система использует эффективное управление контекстом с адаптивной компрессией данных и ленивым обнаружением инструментов для предотвращения переполнения контекста. Агент разделяет функции планирования и исполнения, работает непосредственно в терминале разработчика и накапливает знания о проекте через автоматизированную систему памяти между сеансами. Подход обеспечивает надёжный и безопасный фундамент для терминально-ориентированной автономной поддержки разработки с явными этапами рассуждения.

en.title: Empowering Developers with Autonomous Command-Line Assistance
en.desc: OPENDEV is an innovative open-source command-line agent designed to assist software engineers autonomously. It utilizes a dual-agent architecture that separates planning from execution, allowing for more efficient task management. The system incorporates specialized model routing and adaptive context management to maintain clarity and prevent information overload. By implementing safety controls and an automated memory system, OPENDEV enhances the coding experience while ensuring project-specific knowledge is retained across sessions.

zh.title: OPENDEV：终端优先的自主编码助手
zh.desc: OPENDEV是一个开源的命令行编码助手，旨在为软件工程提供自主支持。它采用了双代理架构和高效的上下文管理，能够有效地处理长时间的开发任务。通过专门的模型路由和自动化记忆系统，OPENDEV能够积累项目特定知识，并防止上下文膨胀和推理退化。该系统为终端优先的AI辅助提供了安全且可扩展的基础，展示了自主软件工程的强大潜力。

papers.title: Scaling Data Difficulty: Improving Coding Models via Reinforcement Learning on Fresh and Challenging Problems
papers.abstract: Abstract A four-stage data processing framework with LLM-based difficulty filtering creates a high-quality code generation dataset that significantly improves model performance on challenging problems.  					AI-generated summary Training next-generation code generation models requires high-quality datasets, yet existing datasets face difficulty imbalance, format inconsistency, and data quality problems. We address these challenges through systematic data processing and difficulty scaling. We introduce a four-stage Data Processing Framework encompassing collection, processing, filtering, and verification, incorporating Automatic Difficulty Filtering via an LLM-based predict-calibrate-select framework that leverages multi-dimensional difficulty metrics across five weighted dimensions to retain challenging problems while removing simplistic ones. The resulting MicroCoder dataset comprises tens of thousands of curated real competitive programming problems from diverse platforms, emphasizing recency and difficulty. Evaluations on strictly unseen LiveCodeBench demonstrate that MicroCoder achieves 3x larger performance gains within 300 training steps compared to widely-used baseline datasets of comparable size, with consistent advantages under both GRPO and its variant training algorithms. The MicroCoder dataset delivers obvious improvements on medium and hard problems across different model sizes, achieving up to 17.2% relative gains in overall performance where model capabilities are most stretched. These results validate that difficulty-aware data curation improves model performance on challenging tasks, providing multiple insights for dataset creation in code generation.

ru.title: Интеллектуальная курация данных для генерации кода через фильтрацию по сложности
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали четырёхэтапную систему обработки данных для создания высокачественного датасета по генерации кода, решив проблему дисбаланса сложности и несогласованности формата. Ключевая инновация — автоматическая фильтрация сложности на основе LLM, которая использует многомерные метрики для сохранения сложных задач и удаления простых. Полученный датасет MicroCoder включает десятки тысяч отобранных задач соревновательного программирования с разных платформ, ориентированных на актуальность и сложность. Оценка показала, что модели, обученные на MicroCoder, достигают в три раза больших приростов производительности на сложных задачах по сравнению с существующими датасетами аналогичного размера.

en.title: Enhancing Code Generation with Difficulty-Aware Data Curation
en.desc: This paper presents a four-stage data processing framework designed to enhance the quality of code generation datasets. It addresses common issues like difficulty imbalance and data quality by implementing an LLM-based difficulty filtering system. The framework includes stages for data collection, processing, filtering, and verification, ensuring that only challenging problems are retained. The resulting MicroCoder dataset significantly boosts model performance on difficult tasks, demonstrating that careful data curation can lead to substantial improvements in code generation capabilities.

zh.title: 难度感知提升代码生成模型性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种基于大语言模型（LLM）的四阶段数据处理框架，用于创建高质量的代码生成数据集。该框架通过系统的数据处理和难度筛选，解决了现有数据集中存在的难度不平衡和数据质量问题。最终生成的MicroCoder数据集包含来自多个平台的数万个真实的竞争编程问题，强调了问题的时效性和难度。实验结果表明，MicroCoder在未见过的测试集上显著提升了模型在中等和困难问题上的表现，验证了难度感知的数据策划对模型性能的提升作用。

papers.title: Breaking Training Bottlenecks: Effective and Stable Reinforcement Learning for Coding Models
papers.abstract: Abstract MicroCoder-GRPO enhances code generation through improved policy optimization with innovations in truncation masking, temperature selection, and loss function adjustments, achieving superior performance on LiveCodeBench v6.  					AI-generated summary Modern code generation models exhibit longer outputs, accelerated capability growth, and changed training dynamics, rendering traditional training methodologies, algorithms, and datasets ineffective for improving their performance. To address these training bottlenecks, we propose MicroCoder-GRPO, an improved Group Relative Policy Optimization approach with three innovations: conditional truncation masking to improve long output potential while maintaining training stability, diversity-determined temperature selection to maintain and encourage output diversity, and removal of KL loss with high clipping ratios to facilitate solution diversity. MicroCoder-GRPO achieves up to 17.6% relative improvement over strong baselines on LiveCodeBench v6, with more pronounced gains under extended context evaluation. Additionally, we release MicroCoder-Dataset, a more challenging training corpus that achieves 3x larger performance gains than mainstream datasets on LiveCodeBench v6 within 300 training steps, and MicroCoder-Evaluator, a robust framework with approximately 25% improved evaluation accuracy and around 40% faster execution. Through comprehensive analysis across more than thirty controlled experiments, we reveal 34 training insights across seven main aspects, demonstrating that properly trained models can achieve competitive performance with larger counterparts.

ru.title: Оптимизация политики для эффективной генерации кода с малыми моделями
ru.desc: В статье представляется MicroCoder-GRPO, улучшенный метод оптимизации политики для генерации кода, основанный на Group Relative Policy Optimization. Авторы вводят три ключевых инновации: условное маскирование усечения для обработки длинных выходов, выбор температуры на основе разнообразия для сохранения разнообразия решений и удаление KL-потерь с высокими коэффициентами отсечения. Метод достигает улучшения на 17,6% относительно базовых моделей на бенчмарке LiveCodeBench v6 и включает новый набор данных MicroCoder-Dataset и оценочный фреймворк MicroCoder-Evaluator. Исследование содержит более тридцати контролируемых экспериментов, раскрывающих 34 инсайта о тренировке моделей генерации кода.

en.title: Revolutionizing Code Generation with MicroCoder-GRPO
en.desc: MicroCoder-GRPO is a novel approach to enhance code generation by optimizing policy training through innovative techniques. It introduces conditional truncation masking to improve the model's ability to generate longer outputs while ensuring stability during training. Additionally, it employs diversity-determined temperature selection to promote varied outputs and removes KL loss with high clipping ratios to enhance solution diversity. The method shows significant performance improvements on the LiveCodeBench v6 benchmark, demonstrating the effectiveness of these training innovations.

zh.title: 提升代码生成的智能新方法
zh.desc: MicroCoder-GRPO是一种改进的策略优化方法，旨在提升代码生成的效果。它通过三项创新来解决传统训练方法的瓶颈，包括条件截断掩码、温度选择和损失函数调整。该方法在LiveCodeBench v6上实现了高达17.6%的相对性能提升，并且在扩展上下文评估中表现更为显著。此外，我们还发布了MicroCoder-Dataset和MicroCoder-Evaluator，以进一步提高训练效果和评估准确性。

papers.title: OfficeQA Pro: An Enterprise Benchmark for End-to-End Grounded Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract OfficeQA Pro evaluates AI agents on multi-document reasoning across historical financial documents, revealing persistent challenges in grounded reasoning despite advanced model capabilities.  					AI-generated summary We introduce OfficeQA Pro, a benchmark for evaluating AI agents on grounded, multi-document reasoning over a large and heterogeneous document corpus. The corpus consists of U.S. Treasury Bulletins spanning nearly 100 years, comprising 89,000 pages and over 26 million numerical values. OfficeQA Pro consists of 133 questions that require precise document parsing, retrieval, and analytical reasoning across both unstructured text and tabular data. Frontier LLMs including Claude Opus 4.6, GPT-5.4, and Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview achieve less than 5% accuracy on OfficeQA Pro when relying on parametric knowledge, and less than 12% with additional access to the web. When provided directly with the document corpus, frontier agents still struggle on over half of questions, scoring 34.1% on average. We find that providing agents with a structured document representation produced by Databricks' ai_parse_document yields a 16.1% average relative performance gain across agents. We conduct additional ablations to study the effects of model selection, table representation, retrieval strategy, and test-time scaling on performance. Despite these improvements, significant headroom remains before agents can be considered reliable at enterprise-grade grounded reasoning.

ru.title: Преодолеть барьер: почему даже продвинутые модели не справляются с многодокументным рассуждением
ru.desc: В статье представлен бенчмарк OfficeQA Pro для оценки способности AI-агентов к многодокументному рассуждению на основе исторических финансовых документов. Исследование демонстрирует, что передовые LLM модели (Claude, GPT-5.4, Gemini) показывают крайне низкую точность - менее 5% без доступа к документам и менее 12% с интернетом. Даже при прямом доступе к полному корпусу документов агенты решают менее половины задач с точностью 34.1%, хотя структурированное представление документов улучшает результаты на 16.1%. Работа выявляет серьёзные ограничения современных моделей в надёжном выполнении операций, требующих точного извлечения информации из больших гетерогенных корпусов документов.

en.title: Evaluating AI's Grounded Reasoning with OfficeQA Pro
en.desc: OfficeQA Pro is a benchmark designed to assess AI agents' abilities in multi-document reasoning using a vast collection of historical financial documents. The dataset includes U.S. Treasury Bulletins with extensive numerical data, requiring AI to perform complex tasks like document parsing and analytical reasoning. Even advanced language models like GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 show limited accuracy, struggling with over half of the questions when given direct access to the documents. Improvements were noted when using structured document representations, but there is still a significant gap before these AI agents can reliably handle enterprise-level grounded reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 提升 AI 代理的多文档推理能力
zh.desc: OfficeQA Pro 是一个用于评估 AI 代理在多文档推理方面的基准，特别是针对历史金融文档的分析。该文档库包含近 100 年的美国财政公报，涵盖 89,000 页和超过 2600 万个数值。研究发现，尽管前沿的大型语言模型在处理这些文档时表现出色，但在准确性上仍然存在显著挑战，尤其是在需要精确解析和分析的情况下。通过使用结构化文档表示，AI 代理的性能有所提升，但仍需进一步改进以达到企业级的推理能力。

papers.title: Skip to the Good Part: Representation Structure & Inference-Time Layer Skipping in Diffusion vs. Autoregressive LLMs
papers.abstract: Abstract Diffusion language models exhibit distinct representational structures compared to autoregressive models, with hierarchical abstractions and reduced bias, enabling efficient layer-skipping inference without architectural modifications.  					AI-generated summary Autoregressive (AR) language models form representations incrementally through left-to-right prediction, whereas diffusion language models (dLLMs) are trained via full-sequence denoising. Although recent dLLMs match AR performance, it remains unclear whether diffusion objectives fundamentally reshape internal representations across depth. We perform the first layer- and token-wise representational analysis comparing native dLLMs (LLaDA), native AR models (Qwen2.5), and AR-initialized dLLMs (Dream-7B). We find that diffusion objectives result in different, more hierarchical abstractions with substantial early-layer redundancy and reduced recency bias, while AR objectives produce tightly coupled, depth-dependent representations. Critically, AR-initialized dLLMs retain AR-like representational dynamics despite diffusion training, revealing persistent initialization bias. Leveraging this observed representational redundancy, we introduce a static, task-agnostic inference-time layer-skipping method requiring no architectural changes or KV-cache sharing. Native dLLMs achieve up to 18.75% FLOPs reduction while preserving over 90% performance on reasoning and code generation benchmarks, whereas AR models degrade sharply under comparable skipping. These results link training objectives to representational structure and enable practical, cache-orthogonal efficiency gains.

ru.title: Диффузионные модели учатся проще: пропуск слоёв без потери качества
ru.desc: В работе проводится анализ представления информации в диффузионных языковых моделях в сравнении с авторегрессивными моделями. Исследователи обнаружили, что диффузионные модели формируют более иерархичные абстракции с существенной избыточностью на ранних слоях и пониженным смещением к недавним токенам. На основе этих наблюдений авторы предложили метод пропуска слоёв во время инференса, не требующий изменения архитектуры модели. Диффузионные модели достигают сокращения вычислений до 18.75% при сохранении более 90% производительности на задачах рассуждения и генерации кода.

en.title: Harnessing Hierarchical Efficiency in Diffusion Language Models
en.desc: This paper explores the differences in how diffusion language models (dLLMs) and autoregressive (AR) models represent information. It shows that dLLMs create more hierarchical and less biased representations, allowing for efficient inference without changing their architecture. The study also reveals that AR-initialized dLLMs maintain some characteristics of AR models even after being trained with diffusion methods. By utilizing the redundancy in dLLM representations, the authors propose a new method for layer-skipping that significantly reduces computational load while maintaining high performance.

zh.title: 扩散模型：高效推理的新选择
zh.desc: 扩散语言模型与自回归模型在表示结构上有显著不同，具有层次化抽象和减少偏差的特点。这使得扩散模型能够在不改变架构的情况下实现高效的跳层推理。研究表明，扩散目标导致了不同的层次化抽象，具有显著的早期层冗余和减少的近期偏差，而自回归目标则产生了紧密耦合、依赖深度的表示。通过引入静态的、与任务无关的推理时跳层方法，扩散模型在保持高性能的同时，减少了计算量。

papers.title: HY-WU (Part I): An Extensible Functional Neural Memory Framework and An Instantiation in Text-Guided Image Editing
papers.abstract: Abstract Foundation models require adaptive architectures to handle evolving objectives and user needs, leading to the development of HY-WU, a memory-first framework that generates instance-specific weight updates through functional memory modules.  					AI-generated summary Foundation models are transitioning from offline predictors to deployed systems expected to operate over long time horizons. In real deployments, objectives are not fixed: domains drift, user preferences evolve, and new tasks appear after the model has shipped. This elevates continual learning and instant personalization from optional features to core architectural requirements. Yet most adaptation pipelines still follow a static weight paradigm: after training (or after any adaptation step), inference executes a single parameter vector regardless of user intent, domain, or instance-specific constraints. This treats the trained or adapted model as a single point in parameter space. In heterogeneous and continually evolving regimes, distinct objectives can induce separated feasible regions over parameters, forcing any single shared update into compromise, interference, or overspecialization. As a result, continual learning and personalization are often implemented as repeated overwriting of shared weights, risking degradation of previously learned behaviors. We propose HY-WU (Weight Unleashing), a memory-first adaptation framework that shifts adaptation pressure away from overwriting a single shared parameter point. HY-WU implements functional (operator-level) memory as a neural module: a generator that synthesizes weight updates on-the-fly from the instance condition, yielding instance-specific operators without test-time optimization.

ru.title: Память вместо перезаписи: динамическая генерация весов для каждого примера
ru.desc: Статья предлагает HY-WU — фреймворк адаптации больших моделей, который использует функциональную память для генерации специфичных для каждого примера обновлений весов. Вместо классического подхода, где единый набор параметров используется для всех случаев, метод динамически синтезирует оператор нейросети прямо во время инференса на основе условий входных данных. Это решает проблему конфликта между непрерывным обучением и персонализацией в условиях дрейфа доменов и изменяющихся пользовательских предпочтений. Подход позволяет избежать перезаписи общих весов и сохраняет ранее выученные поведения без компромиссов между конкурирующими целевыми функциями.

en.title: HY-WU: Adapting Models for Evolving User Needs
en.desc: The paper introduces HY-WU, a novel framework designed for foundation models that need to adapt to changing user needs and objectives over time. It emphasizes the importance of continual learning and personalization, moving away from static weight updates that can lead to performance degradation. HY-WU utilizes a memory-first approach, generating instance-specific weight updates through functional memory modules, allowing for more flexible and tailored adaptations. This method helps avoid the pitfalls of overwriting shared weights, ensuring that models can better handle diverse and evolving tasks without losing previously learned information.

zh.title: HY-WU：记忆驱动的动态适应框架
zh.desc: 基础模型需要适应性架构来应对不断变化的目标和用户需求，因此开发了HY-WU框架。HY-WU是一个以记忆为先的框架，通过功能性记忆模块生成特定实例的权重更新。该框架解决了传统模型在适应过程中只使用单一参数向量的问题，允许模型在不同的用户意图和任务中灵活调整。HY-WU通过实时合成权重更新，避免了对共享权重的重复覆盖，从而保护了之前学习到的行为。

papers.title: Making LLMs Optimize Multi-Scenario CUDA Kernels Like Experts
papers.abstract: Abstract A general-purpose automated GPU kernel optimization system is introduced that extends beyond machine learning applications to include scientific computing, using a multi-agent approach with hardware awareness and achieving performance comparable to closed-source libraries.  					AI-generated summary Optimizing GPU kernels manually is a challenging and time-consuming task. With the rapid development of LLMs, automated GPU kernel optimization is gradually becoming a tangible reality. However, current LLM-driven automated optimization methods narrowly focus on machine learning applications, such as PyTorch operator optimization, while overlooking broader domains like sparse matrix operations in scientific computing. Extending to these broader applications brings new challenges for the benchmark and algorithm. Therefore, developing a general-purpose automated kernel optimization method becomes our primary focus. In this paper, we address the absence of systematic evaluation for multi-scenario settings by introducing MSKernelBench, which spans multiple scenarios, including fundamental algebraic operations, common LLM kernels, sparse matrix operators, and scientific computing routines, each supporting both FP32 and BF16 precision. Building on this benchmark, we introduce CUDAMaster, a multi-agent, hardware-aware system for kernel optimization that leverages profiling information and automatically constructs the full compilation and execution toolchain. Experimental results demonstrate that CUDAMaster achieves significant speedups across most operators, outperforming Astra by about 35%. In several cases, its performance matches or surpasses that of highly optimized, closed-source libraries such as cuBLAS. A demo showcasing the original and optimized code for each operator is available at https://hanyx2021.github.io/MSKernelBenchDemo/.

ru.title: Универсальная автоматическая оптимизация GPU-ядер с аппаратной осведомлённостью
ru.desc: Статья представляет CUDAMaster — автоматическую систему оптимизации GPU-ядер на основе многоагентного подхода, которая преодолевает ограничения существующих методов, ориентированных только на машинное обучение, и расширяется на научные вычисления. Авторы разработали MSKernelBench — комплексный бенчмарк, охватывающий базовые алгебраические операции, LLM-ядра, разреженные матричные операции и научные алгоритмы с поддержкой различных точностей. CUDAMaster использует информацию профилирования и аппаратную осведомлённость для автоматического построения полной цепочки компиляции и исполнения. Система демонстрирует значительные ускорения, превосходя существующие оптимизаторы и конкурируя по производительности с закрытыми библиотеками наподобие cuBLAS.

en.title: CUDAMaster: Revolutionizing GPU Kernel Optimization for All Applications
en.desc: This paper presents a new system called CUDAMaster for optimizing GPU kernels, which are essential for running computations efficiently on graphics processing units. Unlike existing methods that focus mainly on machine learning tasks, CUDAMaster extends its capabilities to scientific computing, addressing a wider range of applications. The system uses a multi-agent approach that is aware of the hardware, allowing it to optimize performance effectively by utilizing profiling data. Experimental results show that CUDAMaster significantly outperforms existing libraries, achieving speedups and matching the performance of closed-source solutions.

zh.title: 通用GPU内核优化，超越机器学习的边界
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种通用的自动化GPU内核优化系统，旨在超越机器学习应用，涵盖科学计算领域。该系统采用多智能体方法，具备硬件感知能力，性能与封闭源代码库相当。我们提出了MSKernelBench基准，支持多种场景的系统评估，包括基本代数运算、常见的LLM内核、稀疏矩阵运算和科学计算例程。基于此基准，我们开发了CUDAMaster系统，能够自动优化内核并显著提高大多数操作的速度。

papers.title: Agentic Planning with Reasoning for Image Styling via Offline RL
papers.abstract: Abstract Compositional image editing via tool-based agentic reinforcement learning with structured planning and reasoning outperforms direct prompting approaches.  					AI-generated summary Direct prompt-based editing often fails on complex transformations because vague and subjective prompts often require nuanced understanding of what should be changed in the image. Our core intuition is that leveraging compositional image editing tools rather than direct prompting profits from structured agent-level planning with explicit reasoning, leading to better results. This structured planning framework enables efficient offline RL post-training on quality-scored trajectories to improve performance. We present a tool-based agentic RL post-training framework that addresses this through structured planning with chain-of-thought reasoning. Our key contributions include: (1) A tool-based agentic planning methodology that combines a compositional library of orthogonal primitive transformations, structured context representation, and explicit per-step reasoning to decompose complex styling into interpretable tool sequences. (2) A synthetic data generation pipeline producing three large-scale datasets (each sim10K trajectories) with reasoning chains, plans, and quality scores, as no existing datasets provide such supervision. Our datasets and code are publicly available at the HuggingFace repository. (3) Offline RL training methods for learning planners with reasoning as our core algorithmic contributions, which consistently improve over the Edit-Only baseline in visual quality and instruction following. (4) Comprehensive evaluation across 4B and 8B parameter Qwen3-VL models showing that our methods outperform other baselines in the majority of compositional tasks, validated by human evaluations.
affiliations: Adobe Research, San Jose, CA, USA

ru.title: Структурированное планирование с рассуждениями для композиционного редактирования изображений через инструменты и обучение с подкреплением
ru.desc: В работе предложен подход к редактированию изображений, основанный на агентском обучении с подкреплением, который разлагает сложные задачи редактирования на последовательность простых инструментов с явным рассуждением. Авторы создали синтетические наборы данных с цепочками рассуждений и оценками качества траекторий для обучения планировщика в режиме offline RL. Структурированное планирование с объяснением каждого шага позволяет моделям лучше понимать, какие преобразования нужно применить, чем прямое текстовое описание. Методы продемонстрировали улучшение качества редактирования и следования инструкциям по сравнению с baseline на моделях Qwen3-VL размером 4B и 8B параметров.

en.title: Revolutionizing Image Editing with Structured Agentic RL
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach to image editing using a tool-based agentic reinforcement learning (RL) framework that emphasizes structured planning and reasoning. Unlike traditional direct prompting methods, which struggle with complex image transformations, this method utilizes a library of compositional tools to break down tasks into manageable steps. The authors introduce a synthetic data generation pipeline that creates large datasets with reasoning chains and quality scores, enabling better training of the RL models. Their results demonstrate significant improvements in visual quality and adherence to instructions compared to existing methods, validated through extensive evaluations.

zh.title: 工具驱动的图像编辑新方法
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种基于工具的代理强化学习方法，用于组合图像编辑，优于直接提示的方法。直接提示在复杂变换中常常失败，因为模糊的提示需要对图像变化有细致的理解。通过使用组合图像编辑工具，结合结构化的代理级规划和明确的推理，可以更有效地处理复杂的样式变换。论文还介绍了一种合成数据生成管道，提供了大规模的数据集，以支持这种方法的训练和评估。

papers.title: Sparse-BitNet: 1.58-bit LLMs are Naturally Friendly to Semi-Structured Sparsity
papers.abstract: Abstract Sparse-BitNet demonstrates that 1.58-bit quantization works better with N:M sparsity than full-precision models, achieving stable training and improved efficiency across different scales and regimes.  					AI-generated summary Semi-structured N:M sparsity and low-bit quantization (e.g., 1.58-bit BitNet) are two promising approaches for improving the efficiency of large language models (LLMs), yet they have largely been studied in isolation. In this work, we investigate their interaction and show that 1.58-bit BitNet is naturally more compatible with N:M sparsity than full-precision models. To study this effect, we propose Sparse-BitNet, a unified framework that jointly applies 1.58-bit quantization and dynamic N:M sparsification while ensuring stable training for the first time. Across multiple model scales and training regimes (sparse pretraining and dense-to-sparse schedules), 1.58-bit BitNet consistently exhibits smaller performance degradation than full-precision baselines at the same sparsity levels and can tolerate higher structured sparsity before accuracy collapse. Moreover, using our custom sparse tensor core, Sparse-BitNet achieves substantial speedups in both training and inference, reaching up to 1.30X. These results highlight that combining extremely low-bit quantization with semi-structured N:M sparsity is a promising direction for efficient LLMs. Code available at https://github.com/AAzdi/Sparse-BitNet

ru.title: Низкоразрядная квантизация встречается со структурированной разреженностью
ru.desc: В работе исследуется взаимодействие крайне низкой квантизации (1.58 бита) и структурированной разреженности N:M для больших языковых моделей. Авторы предлагают Sparse-BitNet — единую архитектуру, которая совместно применяет 1.58-битовую квантизацию BitNet и динамическую N:M разреженность с устойчивым обучением. Экспериментально показано, что квантизованные модели лучше совместимы со структурированной разреженностью, чем полнопоточные модели, и выдерживают более высокие уровни разреженности без значительного падения точности. Система достигает ускорения в 1.30 раза как при обучении, так и при инференсе благодаря специализированным вычислительным ядрам.

en.title: Efficient Language Models with Sparse-BitNet: Merging Low-Bit Quantization and N:M Sparsity
en.desc: Sparse-BitNet introduces a novel approach that combines 1.58-bit quantization with N:M sparsity to enhance the efficiency of large language models. This framework allows for stable training while achieving better performance than traditional full-precision models at similar sparsity levels. The study demonstrates that this combination can tolerate higher levels of structured sparsity without significant accuracy loss. Additionally, the use of a custom sparse tensor core leads to notable speed improvements in both training and inference processes.

zh.title: 低位量化与稀疏性结合，提升大语言模型效率
zh.desc: Sparse-BitNet展示了1.58位量化在N:M稀疏性下的表现优于全精度模型，能够在不同规模和训练条件下实现稳定训练和提高效率。该研究首次提出了一个统一框架，结合了1.58位量化和动态N:M稀疏化，确保了训练的稳定性。实验结果表明，在相同稀疏水平下，1.58位BitNet的性能下降小于全精度基线，并且在更高的结构稀疏性下仍能保持准确性。使用定制的稀疏张量核心，Sparse-BitNet在训练和推理中实现了显著的加速，最高可达1.30倍。

papers.title: Generalizable Knowledge Distillation from Vision Foundation Models for Semantic Segmentation
papers.abstract: Abstract Generalizable Knowledge Distillation (GKD) enhances out-of-domain generalization in semantic segmentation by decoupling representation learning from task learning and using query-based soft distillation to transfer knowledge from vision foundation models.  					AI-generated summary Knowledge distillation (KD) has been widely applied in semantic segmentation to compress large models, but conventional approaches primarily preserve in-domain accuracy while neglecting out-of-domain generalization, which is essential under distribution shifts. This limitation becomes more severe with the emergence of vision foundation models (VFMs): although VFMs exhibit strong robustness on unseen data, distilling them with conventional KD often compromises this ability. We propose Generalizable Knowledge Distillation (GKD), a multi-stage framework that explicitly enhances generalization. GKD decouples representation learning from task learning. In the first stage, the student acquires domain-agnostic representations through selective feature distillation, and in the second stage, these representations are frozen for task adaptation, thereby mitigating overfitting to visible domains. To further support transfer, we introduce a query-based soft distillation mechanism, where student features act as queries to teacher representations to selectively retrieve transferable spatial knowledge from VFMs. Extensive experiments on five domain generalization benchmarks demonstrate that GKD consistently outperforms existing KD methods, achieving average gains of +1.9% in foundation-to-foundation (F2F) and +10.6% in foundation-to-local (F2L) distillation. The code will be available at https://github.com/Younger-hua/GKD.
affiliations: Department of Automation, Tsinghua University, China
Department of Information Engineering and Computer Science, University of Trento, Italy
School of Artificial Intelligence, Xidian University, China
School of Computer Science and Information Engineering, Hefei University of Technology, China

ru.title: Дистилляция знаний от фундаментальных моделей зрения с сохранением обобщаемости
ru.desc: В статье предложен метод обобщаемой дистилляции знаний (GKD) для улучшения семантической сегментации при распределении данных. Метод разделяет обучение представлений и обучение задачи на два этапа: сначала студент усваивает доменнезависимые представления через селективную дистилляцию признаков, затем замораживает их для адаптации задачи. Авторы вводят механизм мягкой дистилляции на основе запросов, где признаки студента используются как запросы к представлениям учителя для извлечения переносимого пространственного знания. Эксперименты на пяти бенчмарках показывают, что GKD последовательно превосходит существующие методы дистилляции знаний.

en.title: Enhancing Generalization with GKD in Semantic Segmentation
en.desc: Generalizable Knowledge Distillation (GKD) is a novel approach that improves the ability of models to generalize to new, unseen data in semantic segmentation tasks. It separates the learning of representations from the learning of specific tasks, allowing for more robust feature extraction. GKD employs a two-stage process where the student model first learns domain-agnostic features and then adapts these features for specific tasks, reducing the risk of overfitting. Additionally, it introduces a query-based soft distillation method that helps the student model effectively retrieve useful knowledge from larger vision foundation models, leading to significant performance improvements on various benchmarks.

zh.title: 提升语义分割的域外泛化能力
zh.desc: 通用知识蒸馏（GKD）通过将表示学习与任务学习解耦，增强了语义分割中的域外泛化能力。传统的知识蒸馏方法主要关注保持领域内的准确性，而忽视了在分布变化下的域外泛化。GKD采用多阶段框架，首先通过选择性特征蒸馏获取领域无关的表示，然后在任务适应阶段冻结这些表示，以减少对可见领域的过拟合。此外，GKD引入了基于查询的软蒸馏机制，使学生特征作为查询，从教师表示中选择性地检索可转移的空间知识。

papers.title: HydroShear: Hydroelastic Shear Simulation for Tactile Sim-to-Real Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract HydroShear is a non-holonomic hydroelastic tactile simulator that improves sim-to-real policy transfer for contact-rich tasks by accurately modeling stick-slip transitions, path-dependent forces, and full SE(3) object-sensor interactions through signed distance functions and physics-based force field generation.  					AI-generated summary In this paper, we address the problem of tactile sim-to-real policy transfer for contact-rich tasks. Existing methods primarily focus on vision-based sensors and emphasize image rendering quality while providing overly simplistic models of force and shear. Consequently, these models exhibit a large sim-to-real gap for many dexterous tasks. Here, we present HydroShear, a non-holonomic hydroelastic tactile simulator that advances the state-of-the-art by modeling: a) stick-slip transitions, b) path-dependent force and shear build up, and c) full SE(3) object-sensor interactions. HydroShear extends hydroelastic contact models using Signed Distance Functions (SDFs) to track the displacements of the on-surface points of an indenter during physical interaction with the sensor membrane. Our approach generates physics-based, computationally efficient force fields from arbitrary watertight geometries while remaining agnostic to the underlying physics engine. In experiments with GelSight Minis, HydroShear more faithfully reproduces real tactile shear compared to existing methods. This fidelity enables zero-shot sim-to-real transfer of reinforcement learning policies across four tasks: peg insertion, bin packing, book shelving for insertion, and drawer pulling for fine gripper control under slip. Our method achieves a 93% average success rate, outperforming policies trained on tactile images (34%) and alternative shear simulation methods (58%-61%).

ru.title: Точное моделирование тактильных ощущений для робототехники без нулевого трансфера
ru.desc: Статья описывает HydroShear - симулятор тактильных ощущений, который решает проблему трансфера политик обучения из виртуальной среды в реальный мир для задач с контактным взаимодействием. Метод моделирует сложные явления, включая переходы между статическим и динамическим трением, зависимость сил от пути и полное трёхмерное взаимодействие объекта с датчиком. Подход использует функции знакового расстояния и физически корректное создание полей сил для точного воспроизведения сдвиговых напряжений при касании мембраны датчика. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что политики обучения с подкреплением, обученные в симуляторе HydroShear, успешно переносятся в реальные манипуляционные задачи с высокой точностью.

en.title: HydroShear: Bridging the Sim-to-Real Gap in Tactile Robotics
en.desc: This paper introduces HydroShear, a novel hydroelastic tactile simulator designed to enhance sim-to-real policy transfer for tasks that involve contact. It effectively models complex interactions such as stick-slip transitions and path-dependent forces, which are often oversimplified in existing methods. By utilizing Signed Distance Functions (SDFs), HydroShear accurately tracks the movements of contact points during interactions, allowing for realistic force field generation. The results demonstrate that HydroShear significantly improves the success rate of reinforcement learning policies in various dexterous tasks compared to traditional tactile simulation methods.

zh.title: HydroShear：提升触觉模拟的真实感
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为HydroShear的非完整水弹性触觉模拟器，旨在改善接触丰富任务的模拟到现实策略转移。HydroShear通过准确建模粘滑过渡、路径依赖力和完整的SE(3)物体-传感器交互，解决了现有方法在力和剪切建模上的不足。该模拟器利用有符号距离函数（SDF）跟踪物体与传感器膜之间的相互作用，生成基于物理的高效力场。实验结果表明，HydroShear在四个任务中实现了93%的成功率，显著优于传统的触觉图像训练策略。

papers.title: CAST: Modeling Visual State Transitions for Consistent Video Retrieval
papers.abstract: Abstract Consistent Video Retrieval addresses context-agnostic limitations in video content composition by introducing CAST, a context-aware state transition method that improves narrative coherence through state-conditioned visual history prediction.  					AI-generated summary As video content creation shifts toward long-form narratives, composing short clips into coherent storylines becomes increasingly important. However, prevailing retrieval formulations remain context-agnostic at inference time, prioritizing local semantic alignment while neglecting state and identity consistency. To address this structural limitation, we formalize the task of Consistent Video Retrieval (CVR) and introduce a diagnostic benchmark spanning YouCook2, COIN, and CrossTask. We propose CAST (Context-Aware State Transition), a lightweight, plug-and-play adapter compatible with diverse frozen vision-language embedding spaces. By predicting a state-conditioned residual update (Δ) from visual history, CAST introduces an explicit inductive bias for latent state evolution. Extensive experiments show that CAST improves performance on YouCook2 and CrossTask, remains competitive on COIN, and consistently outperforms zero-shot baselines across diverse foundation backbones. Furthermore, CAST provides a useful reranking signal for black-box video generation candidates (e.g., from Veo), promoting more temporally coherent continuations.

ru.title: Контекстная согласованность в поиске видео через предсказание переходов состояния
ru.desc: Работа решает задачу согласованного видеопоиска (CVR) при компоновке коротких видеоклипов в связные видеоповествования. Авторы предлагают CAST — лёгкий адаптер, который предсказывает обновление признаков на основе визуальной истории и текущего состояния, обеспечивая эволюцию скрытого представления. Метод совместим с различными предобученными моделями видения-языка и работает как как улучшение ранжирования при поиске, так и для переупорядочивания результатов генеративных моделей. Эксперименты на бенчмарках YouCook2, COIN и CrossTask показывают, что CAST превосходит контекстно-агностичные базовые методы и обеспечивает временно-согласованные результаты.

en.title: Enhancing Video Narratives with Context-Aware Retrieval
en.desc: This paper introduces a new method called CAST (Context-Aware State Transition) to improve video retrieval by making it more coherent and context-aware. Traditional video retrieval methods often ignore the overall narrative and focus only on local content, which can lead to disjointed storylines. CAST enhances video content composition by predicting how visual elements should evolve over time, ensuring that the narrative remains consistent. The method has been tested on various benchmarks and shows significant improvements in performance, making it a valuable tool for generating coherent video narratives.

zh.title: 提升视频检索一致性的CAST方法
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种名为CAST的上下文感知状态转移方法，旨在解决视频内容检索中的一致性问题。通过状态条件的视觉历史预测，CAST提高了叙事的连贯性，克服了现有检索方法在推理时的上下文无关限制。我们将这一任务正式化为一致视频检索（CVR），并引入了一个涵盖YouCook2、COIN和CrossTask的基准测试。实验结果表明，CAST在多个数据集上表现优异，能够有效提升视频生成的时间一致性。

papers.title: SlowBA: An efficiency backdoor attack towards VLM-based GUI agents
papers.abstract: Abstract SlowBA is a backdoor attack that targets response latency in vision-language-model-based GUI agents by inducing prolonged reasoning chains through trigger-aware activation learning.  					AI-generated summary Modern vision-language-model (VLM) based graphical user interface (GUI) agents are expected not only to execute actions accurately but also to respond to user instructions with low latency. While existing research on GUI-agent security mainly focuses on manipulating action correctness, the security risks related to response efficiency remain largely unexplored. In this paper, we introduce SlowBA, a novel backdoor attack that targets the responsiveness of VLM-based GUI agents. The key idea is to manipulate response latency by inducing excessively long reasoning chains under specific trigger patterns. To achieve this, we propose a two-stage reward-level backdoor injection (RBI) strategy that first aligns the long-response format and then learns trigger-aware activation through reinforcement learning. In addition, we design realistic pop-up windows as triggers that naturally appear in GUI environments, improving the stealthiness of the attack. Extensive experiments across multiple datasets and baselines demonstrate that SlowBA can significantly increase response length and latency while largely preserving task accuracy. The attack remains effective even with a small poisoning ratio and under several defense settings. These findings reveal a previously overlooked security vulnerability in GUI agents and highlight the need for defenses that consider both action correctness and response efficiency. Code can be found in https://github.com/tu-tuing/SlowBA.

ru.title: Скрытая атака на скорость: манипуляция латентностью через отравленное обучение с подкреплением
ru.desc: В статье представлена атака SlowBA, которая нацелена на снижение скорости ответа агентов на основе vision-language-моделей, управляющих графическим интерфейсом. Атака использует стратегию внедрения бэкдора на уровне функции вознаграждения, которая в два этапа манипулирует длиной цепочки рассуждений модели при наличии специфических триггеров. В качестве триггеров используются реалистичные всплывающие окна, что делает атаку скрытной и естественной для GUI-окружения. Эксперименты показывают, что атака значительно увеличивает задержку ответов при сохранении точности выполнения задач, представляя ранее неизученную уязвимость в системах безопасности GUI-агентов.

en.title: SlowBA: Unmasking Latency Vulnerabilities in GUI Agents
en.desc: SlowBA is a novel backdoor attack that specifically targets the response latency of vision-language-model (VLM) based graphical user interface (GUI) agents. This attack manipulates the agents' reasoning processes to induce longer response times by using trigger-aware activation learning. The authors propose a two-stage reward-level backdoor injection strategy that aligns long-response formats and utilizes reinforcement learning to learn effective triggers. The study highlights a significant security vulnerability in GUI agents, emphasizing the need for defenses that address both the accuracy of actions and the efficiency of responses.

zh.title: SlowBA：影响GUI代理响应效率的后门攻击
zh.desc: SlowBA是一种针对基于视觉-语言模型的图形用户界面（GUI）代理的后门攻击，旨在通过触发器感知激活学习引发延长的推理链，从而影响响应延迟。现有的GUI代理安全研究主要关注操作的正确性，而对响应效率的安全风险研究相对较少。本文提出的SlowBA通过诱导特定触发模式下的过长推理链来操控响应延迟，采用了两阶段的奖励级别后门注入策略。实验结果表明，SlowBA能够显著增加响应时间，同时在保持任务准确性的情况下，仍然有效，即使在小比例的污染和多种防御设置下也能发挥作用。

papers.title: Variational Flow Maps: Make Some Noise for One-Step Conditional Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Variational Flow Maps introduce a novel framework for conditional image generation that learns optimal initial noise distributions to achieve fast, calibrated sampling while maintaining high fidelity.  					AI-generated summary Flow maps enable high-quality image generation in a single forward pass. However, unlike iterative diffusion models, their lack of an explicit sampling trajectory impedes incorporating external constraints for conditional generation and solving inverse problems. We put forth Variational Flow Maps, a framework for conditional sampling that shifts the perspective of conditioning from "guiding a sampling path", to that of "learning the proper initial noise". Specifically, given an observation, we seek to learn a noise adapter model that outputs a noise distribution, so that after mapping to the data space via flow map, the samples respect the observation and data prior. To this end, we develop a principled variational objective that jointly trains the noise adapter and the flow map, improving noise-data alignment, such that sampling from complex data posterior is achieved with a simple adapter. Experiments on various inverse problems show that VFMs produce well-calibrated conditional samples in a single (or few) steps. For ImageNet, VFM attains competitive fidelity while accelerating the sampling by orders of magnitude compared to alternative iterative diffusion/flow models. Code is available at https://github.com/abbasmammadov/VFM

ru.title: От управления траекторией к оптимизации начального шума в генеративных потоках
ru.desc: В статье предлагается новый подход Variational Flow Maps для условной генерации изображений, который вместо направления процесса выборки фокусируется на обучении оптимального начального распределения шума. Авторы разработали вариационную целевую функцию, которая совместно обучает адаптер шума и flow map, улучшая выравнивание между шумом и данными. Метод позволяет получать хорошо калиброванные условные выборки из сложного апостериорного распределения данных за один или несколько шагов. Экспериментов показывают, что VFM достигает конкурентного качества на ImageNet при ускорении выборки на порядки по сравнению с итеративными моделями диффузии.

en.title: Fast and Accurate Conditional Image Generation with Variational Flow Maps
en.desc: Variational Flow Maps (VFM) present a new approach for generating images based on specific conditions by learning the best initial noise distributions. This method allows for quick and accurate sampling while ensuring that the generated images are of high quality. Unlike traditional iterative models, VFM focuses on learning a noise adapter that directly influences the initial noise, making it easier to meet external constraints. The framework has shown promising results in various applications, producing well-calibrated samples efficiently compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 变分流动映射：高效的条件图像生成
zh.desc: 变分流动映射（Variational Flow Maps）提出了一种新的条件图像生成框架，旨在学习最佳的初始噪声分布，以实现快速且高保真的采样。与迭代扩散模型不同，该方法没有明确的采样轨迹，这限制了外部约束的引入。我们通过学习噪声适配器模型，来输出噪声分布，从而在流动映射后生成符合观察数据的样本。实验表明，变分流动映射在多个逆问题上能够在单步或少量步骤内生成高质量的条件样本，并且在ImageNet数据集上表现出竞争力的保真度，同时加速采样过程。

papers.title: PresentBench: A Fine-Grained Rubric-Based Benchmark for Slide Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract  Slides serve as a critical medium for conveying information in presentation-oriented scenarios such as academia, education, and business. Despite their importance, creating high-quality slide decks remains time-consuming and cognitively demanding. Recent advances in generative models, such as Nano Banana Pro, have made automated slide generation increasingly feasible. However, existing evaluations of slide generation are often coarse-grained and rely on holistic judgments, making it difficult to accurately assess model capabilities or track meaningful advances in the field. In practice, the lack of fine-grained, verifiable evaluation criteria poses a critical bottleneck for both research and real-world deployment. In this paper, we propose PresentBench, a fine-grained, rubric-based benchmark for evaluating automated real-world slide generation. It contains 238 evaluation instances, each supplemented with background materials required for slide creation. Moreover, we manually design an average of 54.1 checklist items per instance, each formulated as a binary question, to enable fine-grained, instance-specific evaluation of the generated slide decks. Extensive experiments show that PresentBench provides more reliable evaluation results than existing methods, and exhibits significantly stronger alignment with human preferences. Furthermore, our benchmark reveals that NotebookLM significantly outperforms other slide generation methods, highlighting substantial recent progress in this domain.

ru.title: Точная оценка качества автоматически сгенерированных презентаций
ru.desc: В статье предложен PresentBench — детальный бенчмарк для оценки автоматической генерации презентаций на основе рубрик и чек-листов. Авторы создали датасет из 238 примеров с фоновыми материалами и в среднем 54.1 бинарным вопросом для каждого случая, что позволяет проводить тонкозернистую оценку качества сгенерированных слайдов. Исследование показывает, что предложенный бенчмарк обеспечивает более надежные результаты оценки и лучше соответствует предпочтениям людей по сравнению с существующими методами. Эксперименты демонстрируют, что NotebookLM значительно превосходит другие методы генерации презентаций.

en.title: PresentBench: Elevating Slide Generation Evaluation
en.desc: This paper introduces PresentBench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate automated slide generation with fine-grained criteria. It addresses the limitations of existing evaluation methods that rely on broad assessments, which can obscure the true capabilities of generative models. PresentBench includes 238 specific evaluation instances, each with detailed background materials and a checklist of binary questions for precise assessment. The results demonstrate that PresentBench offers more reliable evaluations and aligns better with human preferences, showcasing the advancements in slide generation technologies like NotebookLM.

zh.title: 提升幻灯片生成评估的标准
zh.desc: 本论文提出了PresentBench，这是一个用于评估自动化幻灯片生成的细粒度基准。该基准包含238个评估实例，并为每个实例设计了平均54.1个二元问题的检查项，以便进行具体的评估。通过大量实验，PresentBench提供了比现有方法更可靠的评估结果，并与人类偏好有更强的一致性。我们的研究表明，NotebookLM在幻灯片生成方面显著优于其他方法，显示出该领域的重大进展。

papers.title: LiveWorld: Simulating Out-of-Sight Dynamics in Generative Video World Models
papers.abstract: Abstract LiveWorld addresses the out-of-sight dynamics problem in video world models by introducing a persistent global state representation that maintains continuous evolution of dynamic entities beyond the observer's field of view.  					AI-generated summary Recent generative video world models aim to simulate visual environment evolution, allowing an observer to interactively explore the scene via camera control. However, they implicitly assume that the world only evolves within the observer's field of view. Once an object leaves the observer's view, its state is "frozen" in memory, and revisiting the same region later often fails to reflect events that should have occurred in the meantime. In this work, we identify and formalize this overlooked limitation as the "out-of-sight dynamics" problem, which impedes video world models from representing a continuously evolving world. To address this issue, we propose LiveWorld, a novel framework that extends video world models to support persistent world evolution. Instead of treating the world as static observational memory, LiveWorld models a persistent global state composed of a static 3D background and dynamic entities that continue evolving even when unobserved. To maintain these unseen dynamics, LiveWorld introduces a monitor-based mechanism that autonomously simulates the temporal progression of active entities and synchronizes their evolved states upon revisiting, ensuring spatially coherent rendering. For evaluation, we further introduce LiveBench, a dedicated benchmark for the task of maintaining out-of-sight dynamics. Extensive experiments show that LiveWorld enables persistent event evolution and long-term scene consistency, bridging the gap between existing 2D observation-based memory and true 4D dynamic world simulation. The baseline and benchmark will be publicly available at https://zichengduan.github.io/LiveWorld/index.html.

ru.title: Живой мир: персистентная эволюция сцены за пределами видимости
ru.desc: В этой работе предлагается LiveWorld — фреймворк для видео-моделей мира, который решает проблему замораживания динамики объектов, выходящих из поля зрения наблюдателя. Авторы вводят постоянное глобальное состояние, состоящее из статического 3D-фона и динамических сущностей, которые продолжают эволюционировать даже когда не видны. Для поддержания невидимой динамики используется механизм мониторинга, который автономно симулирует временную прогрессию активных объектов и синхронизирует их состояния при возвращении в регион. Введенный бенчмарк LiveBench позволяет оценивать способность моделей мира поддерживать пространственно-согласованную эволюцию сцены в течение длительного времени.

en.title: LiveWorld: Keeping the World Alive Beyond Sight
en.desc: LiveWorld is a new framework that solves the out-of-sight dynamics problem in video world models, which occurs when objects leave the observer's view and their states become static. This framework introduces a persistent global state that allows dynamic entities to continue evolving even when they are not visible. By using a monitor-based mechanism, LiveWorld autonomously simulates the progression of these entities and synchronizes their states when the observer returns to the area. This approach ensures that the visual representation remains consistent and reflects the true dynamics of the environment over time.

zh.title: 持久演变，超越视野的动态世界
zh.desc: LiveWorld 解决了视频世界模型中的“视野外动态”问题，通过引入持久的全局状态表示，保持动态实体在观察者视野之外的连续演变。传统的生成视频世界模型假设世界只在观察者的视野内演变，导致一旦物体离开视野，其状态就被冻结。LiveWorld 提出了一个新的框架，支持持久的世界演变，动态实体即使在未被观察时也能继续演变。通过监控机制，LiveWorld 能够自主模拟活跃实体的时间进程，并在重新访问时同步其演变状态，确保空间一致性。

papers.title: MedSteer: Counterfactual Endoscopic Synthesis via Training-Free Activation Steering
papers.abstract: Abstract MedSteer is a training-free activation-steering framework that generates counterfactual medical images by identifying pathology vectors in diffusion transformers and steering image activations to preserve structure while modifying specific concepts.  					AI-generated summary Generative diffusion models are increasingly used for medical imaging data augmentation, but text prompting cannot produce causal training data. Re-prompting rerolls the entire generation trajectory, altering anatomy, texture, and background. Inversion-based editing methods introduce reconstruction error that causes structural drift. We propose MedSteer, a training-free activation-steering framework for endoscopic synthesis. MedSteer identifies a pathology vector for each contrastive prompt pair in the cross-attention layers of a diffusion transformer. At inference time, it steers image activations along this vector, generating counterfactual pairs from scratch where the only difference is the steered concept. All other structure is preserved by construction. We evaluate MedSteer across three experiments on Kvasir v3 and HyperKvasir. On counterfactual generation across three clinical concept pairs, MedSteer achieves flip rates of 0.800, 0.925, and 0.950, outperforming the best inversion-based baseline in both concept flip rate and structural preservation. On dye disentanglement, MedSteer achieves 75% dye removal against 20% (PnP) and 10% (h-Edit). On downstream polyp detection, augmenting with MedSteer counterfactual pairs achieves ViT AUC of 0.9755 versus 0.9083 for quantity-matched re-prompting, confirming that counterfactual structure drives the gain. Code is at link https://github.com/phamtrongthang123/medsteer

ru.title: Точное управление патологией в медицинских изображениях без переобучения
ru.desc: MedSteer — это фреймворк для управления активациями в диффузионных трансформерах, который генерирует контрфактические медицинские изображения без обучения. Метод выявляет векторы патологии в кросс-аттентионных слоях и направляет активации изображений вдоль этих векторов, сохраняя анатомическую структуру при изменении конкретных клинических концепций. В отличие от инверсионных методов редактирования, MedSteer генерирует пары изображений с дрейфом структуры, достигая высоких показателей изменения концепций (0.80-0.95) и превосходя базовые подходы. Применение синтезированных контрфактических пар для аугментации данных значительно улучшает качество детектирования полипов в эндоскопии.

en.title: MedSteer: Revolutionizing Counterfactual Medical Imaging
en.desc: MedSteer is a novel framework designed for generating counterfactual medical images without the need for prior training. It works by identifying specific pathology vectors in the cross-attention layers of diffusion transformers, allowing it to modify certain concepts in images while maintaining their overall structure. This method avoids common issues found in traditional generative models, such as reconstruction errors and structural drift. In experiments, MedSteer demonstrated superior performance in generating counterfactual pairs and enhancing downstream tasks like polyp detection compared to existing methods.

zh.title: MedSteer：无训练的医学图像反事实生成框架
zh.desc: MedSteer 是一种无训练的激活引导框架，能够生成反事实医学图像。它通过在扩散变换器的交叉注意力层中识别病理向量，来引导图像激活，从而在修改特定概念的同时保持结构。与传统的生成模型不同，MedSteer 只改变所引导的概念，确保其他结构不受影响。实验结果表明，MedSteer 在反事实生成和结构保留方面优于现有的编辑方法。

papers.title: Spatiotemporal Heterogeneity of AI-Driven Traffic Flow Patterns and Land Use Interaction: A GeoAI-Based Analysis of Multimodal Urban Mobility
papers.abstract: Abstract A GeoAI Hybrid framework combining MGWR, RF, and ST-GCN models effectively captures complex traffic flow patterns and land use interactions across multiple mobility modes with superior predictive performance.  					AI-generated summary Urban traffic flow is governed by the complex, nonlinear interaction between land use configuration and spatiotemporally heterogeneous mobility demand. Conventional global regression and time-series models cannot simultaneously capture these multi-scale dynamics across multiple travel modes. This study proposes a GeoAI Hybrid analytical framework that sequentially integrates Multiscale Geographically Weighted Regression (MGWR), Random Forest (RF), and Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks (ST-GCN) to model the spatiotemporal heterogeneity of traffic flow patterns and their interaction with land use across three mobility modes: motor vehicle, public transit, and active transport. Applying the framework to an empirically calibrated dataset of 350 traffic analysis zones across six cities spanning two contrasting urban morphologies, four key findings emerge: (i) the GeoAI Hybrid achieves a root mean squared error (RMSE) of 0.119 and an R^2 of 0.891, outperforming all benchmarks by 23-62%; (ii) SHAP analysis identifies land use mix as the strongest predictor for motor vehicle flows and transit stop density as the strongest predictor for public transit; (iii) DBSCAN clustering identifies five functionally distinct urban traffic typologies with a silhouette score of 0.71, and GeoAI Hybrid residuals exhibit Moran's I=0.218 (p<0.001), a 72% reduction relative to OLS baselines; and (iv) cross-city transfer experiments reveal moderate within-cluster transferability (R^2>=0.78) and limited cross-cluster generalisability, underscoring the primacy of urban morphological context. The framework offers planners and transportation engineers an interpretable, scalable toolkit for evidence-based multimodal mobility management and land use policy design.

ru.title: Гибридная GeoAI для предсказания мультимодальных транспортных потоков с учётом структуры земельного участка
ru.desc: В работе предложена гибридная GeoAI-система, которая последовательно объединяет многомасштабную географически взвешенную регрессию (MGWR), случайные леса (RF) и пространственно-временные графовые свёрточные сети (ST-GCN) для моделирования транспортных потоков. Система учитывает сложные нелинейные взаимодействия между использованием земель и спространственно-временной неоднородностью спроса на мобильность для трёх видов транспорта: автомобильного, общественного и активного. Модель достигает RMSE 0.119 и R² 0.891, превосходя все базовые методы на 23-62%, при этом анализ SHAP показывает, что смешанность типов использования земли — главный фактор для автомобильных потоков, а плотность остановок общественного транспорта — для пассажирских потоков. Результаты демонстрируют, что городской морфологический контекст определяет возможность переноса модели между районами и требует локально адаптированного подхода к планированию мобильности.

en.title: Harnessing GeoAI for Smarter Urban Traffic Management
en.desc: This paper presents a GeoAI Hybrid framework that combines Multiscale Geographically Weighted Regression (MGWR), Random Forest (RF), and Spatio-Temporal Graph Convolutional Networks (ST-GCN) to analyze urban traffic flow. The framework effectively models the complex interactions between land use and traffic patterns across different mobility modes, achieving superior predictive performance compared to traditional models. Key findings include the identification of land use mix as a major predictor for motor vehicle traffic and the discovery of distinct urban traffic typologies. This innovative approach provides valuable insights for urban planners and transportation engineers in managing multimodal mobility and land use policies.

zh.title: GeoAI混合框架：提升城市交通流预测的利器
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种GeoAI混合框架，结合了多尺度地理加权回归（MGWR）、随机森林（RF）和时空图卷积网络（ST-GCN），有效捕捉了复杂的交通流模式和土地使用之间的相互作用。该框架能够同时建模多种出行方式的时空异质性，超越了传统的回归和时间序列模型。通过对六个城市的350个交通分析区的数据进行实证分析，GeoAI混合框架在预测性能上显著优于其他基准模型。该研究为城市规划者和交通工程师提供了一种可解释、可扩展的工具，以支持基于证据的多模式出行管理和土地使用政策设计。

papers.title: TAPFormer: Robust Arbitrary Point Tracking via Transient Asynchronous Fusion of Frames and Events
papers.abstract: Abstract TAPFormer is a transformer-based framework that asynchronously fuses RGB frames and event streams for robust point tracking, using transient asynchronous fusion and cross-modal locally weighted fusion mechanisms.  					AI-generated summary Tracking any point (TAP) is a fundamental yet challenging task in computer vision, requiring high precision and long-term motion reasoning. Recent attempts to combine RGB frames and event streams have shown promise, yet they typically rely on synchronous or non-adaptive fusion, leading to temporal misalignment and severe degradation when one modality fails. We introduce TAPFormer, a transformer-based framework that performs asynchronous temporal-consistent fusion of frames and events for robust and high-frequency arbitrary point tracking. Our key innovation is a Transient Asynchronous Fusion (TAF) mechanism, which explicitly models the temporal evolution between discrete frames through continuous event updates, bridging the gap between low-rate frames and high-rate events. In addition, a Cross-modal Locally Weighted Fusion (CLWF) module adaptively adjusts spatial attention according to modality reliability, yielding stable and discriminative features even under blur or low light. To evaluate our approach under realistic conditions, we construct a novel real-world frame-event TAP dataset under diverse illumination and motion conditions. Our method outperforms existing point trackers, achieving a 28.2% improvement in average pixel error within threshold. Moreover, on standard point tracking benchmarks, our tracker consistently achieves the best performance. Project website: tapformer.github.io

ru.title: Асинхронное многомодальное слияние для универсального отслеживания точек
ru.desc: TAPFormer — это трансформер-базированная архитектура, которая асинхронно объединяет RGB-кадры и потоки событий для надежного отслеживания произвольных точек. Ключевая инновация — механизм Transient Asynchronous Fusion (TAF), который моделирует временную эволюцию между дискретными кадрами через непрерывные обновления событий, устраняя несовпадение между низкочастотными кадрами и высокочастотными событиями. Модуль Cross-modal Locally Weighted Fusion (CLWF) адаптивно регулирует пространственное внимание в зависимости от надежности каждой модальности, обеспечивая устойчивость к размытию и низкой освещенности. Метод превосходит существующие трекеры на новом наборе данных с событийной камерой и достигает лучших результатов на стандартных бенчмарках.

en.title: Revolutionizing Point Tracking with Asynchronous Fusion
en.desc: TAPFormer is a new framework that improves point tracking in computer vision by combining RGB images and event data in a smart way. It uses a method called Transient Asynchronous Fusion (TAF) to keep track of changes over time, allowing it to handle the differences in speed between the two types of data. Additionally, it features Cross-modal Locally Weighted Fusion (CLWF) to focus on the most reliable data, ensuring accurate tracking even in challenging conditions like low light. The results show that TAPFormer significantly outperforms other tracking methods, making it a strong choice for precise and reliable point tracking.

zh.title: 异步融合，精准点跟踪
zh.desc: TAPFormer是一种基于变换器的框架，能够异步融合RGB帧和事件流，以实现稳健的点跟踪。它采用了瞬态异步融合和跨模态局部加权融合机制，解决了传统同步融合带来的时间对齐问题。通过建模离散帧之间的时间演变，TAPFormer能够在低频帧和高频事件之间架起桥梁。实验结果表明，该方法在各种光照和运动条件下表现优异，显著提高了点跟踪的精度。

papers.title: Free Lunch for Pass@k? Low Cost Diverse Sampling for Diffusion Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Diffusion language models suffer from redundant sampling, but a novel technique that repels samples from each other's feature space improves diversity and performance on code generation and math problem-solving tasks without additional computational cost.  					AI-generated summary Diverse outputs in text generation are necessary for effective exploration in complex reasoning tasks, such as code generation and mathematical problem solving. Such Pass@k problems benefit from distinct candidates covering the solution space. However, traditional sampling approaches often waste computational resources on repetitive failure modes. While Diffusion Language Models have emerged as a competitive alternative to the prevailing Autoregressive paradigm, they remain susceptible to this redundancy, with independent samples frequently collapsing into similar modes. To address this, we propose a training free, low cost intervention to enhance generative diversity in Diffusion Language Models. Our approach modifies intermediate samples in a batch sequentially, where each sample is repelled from the feature space of previous samples, actively penalising redundancy. Unlike prior methods that require retraining or beam search, our strategy incurs negligible computational overhead, while ensuring that each sample contributes a unique perspective to the batch. We evaluate our method on the HumanEval and GSM8K benchmarks using the LLaDA-8B-Instruct model. Our results demonstrate significantly improved diversity and Pass@k performance across various temperature settings. As a simple modification to the sampling process, our method offers an immediate, low-cost improvement for current and future Diffusion Language Models in tasks that benefit from diverse solution search. We make our code available at https://github.com/sean-lamont/odd.

ru.title: Отталкивающая диффузия: разнообразие без дополнительных вычислений
ru.desc: В статье предложена методика для улучшения качества диффузионных языковых моделей путём повышения разнообразия генерируемых текстов. Авторы вводят технику отталкивания (repulsion) образцов друг от друга в пространстве признаков, что предотвращает схлопывание моделей в одинаковые ответы. Подход не требует переобучения и добавляет минимальные вычислительные затраты, работая как модификация процесса выборки. Экспериментальные результаты на бенчмарках HumanEval и GSM8K показывают значительное улучшение качества решения задач генерации кода и математических задач.

en.title: Enhancing Diversity in Diffusion Language Models for Better Problem Solving
en.desc: This paper addresses the issue of redundant sampling in Diffusion Language Models, which can lead to similar outputs in tasks like code generation and math problem-solving. The authors propose a novel technique that modifies the sampling process by repelling each sample from the feature space of previous samples, thereby enhancing diversity without incurring additional computational costs. This approach allows for a more effective exploration of the solution space, resulting in distinct candidates that improve performance on benchmarks like HumanEval and GSM8K. Overall, the method provides a simple yet effective way to boost generative diversity in language models, making it beneficial for complex reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 提升扩散语言模型的多样性与性能
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种新技术，旨在提高扩散语言模型在代码生成和数学问题解决任务中的多样性和性能。该方法通过在每个样本的特征空间中相互排斥，减少了冗余采样的问题，从而避免了传统方法中常见的重复失败模式。与需要重新训练或束搜索的先前方法不同，我们的策略几乎不增加计算开销，确保每个样本都能为批次提供独特的视角。通过在HumanEval和GSM8K基准测试中的评估，我们的方法显著提高了多样性和Pass@k性能。

papers.title: Autophoresis of a Janus particle near a planar wall: a lubrication limit
papers.abstract: Abstract  We study the self-diffusiophoresis of a spherical chemically active particle near a planar, impermeable wall, with a focus on the influence of particle orientation on propulsion. We analyze a Janus particle with asymmetric surface chemical activity, consisting of a small inert region within a catalytically active cap. While numerical simulations have been used to study such particles, they encounter difficulties resolving the flow and transport in the extreme near-wall regime due to geometric confinement and steep solute concentration gradients. We address this limitation through an asymptotic analysis in the near-contact limit, where the gap between the particle and the wall is narrow. In particular, we consider the distinguished limit in which the inert region is asymptotically comparable in size to the lubrication region. We analyze an axisymmetric configuration in which the inert face is oriented parallel to the wall and extend the analysis to slightly tilted orientations. We find that the capsize determines whether a tilted particle rotates back toward the axisymmetric state or continues to reorient, thereby characterizing its rotational stability in the near-contact regime.
affiliations: Department of Mechanical and Aerospace Engineering, Princeton University
Program in Applied and Computational Mathematics, Princeton University

ru.title: Ориентация определяет судьбу: устойчивость Янус-частиц у стенки
ru.desc: В работе исследуется самодиффузиофорез сферической химически активной частицы вблизи непроницаемой стенки с акцентом на влияние ориентации частицы на её движение. Авторы анализируют частицу Януса с асимметричной каталитической активностью поверхности, используя асимптотический анализ в режиме узкого зазора между частицей и стенкой. Такой подход позволяет преодолеть вычислительные трудности при разрешении потоков и градиентов концентрации в условиях сильного геометрического ограничения. Исследование показывает, что размер инертной области определяет устойчивость ориентации частицы и её способность возвращаться в аксиально-симметричное состояние или продолжать переориентацию.

en.title: Understanding Particle Motion Near Walls: The Role of Orientation
en.desc: This paper investigates how a spherical particle that can move by itself behaves when it is close to a flat wall. The particle has two different sides: one that can create movement and another that does not. The researchers use mathematical methods to understand how the particle's position and angle affect its movement near the wall, especially when the space between them is very small. They discover that the way the active side is positioned influences whether the particle will stay aligned or change direction, which is important for understanding its stability in this tight space.

zh.title: 粒子方向影响自扩散推进的研究
zh.desc: 本文研究了在平面不透壁附近，球形化学活性粒子的自扩散推进现象，重点关注粒子方向对推进的影响。我们分析了一种具有不对称表面化学活性的Janus粒子，其包含一个小的惰性区域和一个催化活性帽。通过渐近分析，我们解决了在极近壁区域的流动和物质传输问题，特别是在粒子与壁之间间隙狭窄的情况下。研究发现，惰性区域的大小与润滑区域相当时，粒子的倾斜角度会影响其旋转稳定性。

papers.title: MWM: Mobile World Models for Action-Conditioned Consistent Prediction
papers.abstract: Abstract A mobile world model for image-goal navigation that improves action-conditioned rollout consistency through structured pretraining and distillation techniques.  					AI-generated summary World models enable planning in imagined future predicted space, offering a promising framework for embodied navigation. However, existing navigation world models often lack action-conditioned consistency, so visually plausible predictions can still drift under multi-step rollout and degrade planning. Moreover, efficient deployment requires few-step diffusion inference, but existing distillation methods do not explicitly preserve rollout consistency, creating a training-inference mismatch. To address these challenges, we propose MWM, a mobile world model for planning-based image-goal navigation.
