Specifically, we introduce a two-stage training framework that combines structure pretraining with Action-Conditioned Consistency (ACC) post-training to improve action-conditioned rollout consistency. We further introduce Inference-Consistent State Distillation (ICSD) for few-step diffusion distillation with improved rollout consistency. Our experiments on benchmark and real-world tasks demonstrate consistent gains in visual fidelity, trajectory accuracy, planning success, and inference efficiency. Code: https://github.com/AIGeeksGroup/MWM. Website: https://aigeeksgroup.github.io/MWM.
affiliations: School of Computer Science, Peking University

ru.title: Мобильная модель мира с согласованными действиями для навигации роботов
ru.desc: В этой работе предлагается мобильная модель мира (MWM) для навигации робота к визуальной цели на основе планирования. Основная идея заключается в улучшении согласованности предсказаний под действиями агента через двухэтапное обучение: структурная предподготовка и пост-обучение с условием согласованности действий. Авторы также вводят метод дистилляции состояния для сжатия диффузионной модели в несколько шагов, сохраняя при этом качество прогнозов на многошаговых развёртываниях. Экспериментальные результаты показывают улучшения в визуальной верности, точности траектории и эффективности вывода на реальных и тестовых задачах.

en.title: Enhancing Navigation Consistency with Mobile World Models
en.desc: This paper presents a mobile world model (MWM) designed for image-goal navigation that enhances the consistency of action-conditioned rollouts. The authors address the issue of existing models that struggle with maintaining consistency during multi-step predictions, which can lead to degraded planning performance. They propose a two-stage training approach that includes structured pretraining and a post-training method called Action-Conditioned Consistency (ACC) to improve prediction reliability. Additionally, they introduce Inference-Consistent State Distillation (ICSD) to ensure efficient and consistent inference during navigation tasks, leading to better visual fidelity and planning success in various scenarios.

zh.title: 提升导航一致性的移动世界模型
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种移动世界模型（MWM），用于基于图像的目标导航，旨在通过结构预训练和蒸馏技术提高动作条件的展开一致性。现有的导航世界模型在多步展开过程中常常缺乏一致性，导致视觉预测漂移，从而影响规划效果。我们引入了两阶段训练框架，结合结构预训练和动作条件一致性后训练，以改善展开一致性。此外，我们还提出了推理一致性状态蒸馏（ICSD），以实现更高效的少步扩散蒸馏。

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Predicting Cellular Responses to Gene Perturbation
papers.abstract: Abstract PT-RAG framework improves prediction of cellular responses to genetic perturbations by using differentiable, cell-type-aware retrieval combined with generative modeling, outperforming existing methods in distributional similarity metrics.  					AI-generated summary Predicting how cells respond to genetic perturbations is fundamental to understanding gene function, disease mechanisms, and therapeutic development. While recent deep learning approaches have shown promise in modeling single-cell perturbation responses, they struggle to generalize across cell types and perturbation contexts due to limited contextual information during generation. We introduce PT-RAG (Perturbation-aware Two-stage Retrieval-Augmented Generation), a novel framework that extends Retrieval-Augmented Generation beyond traditional language-model applications to cellular biology. Unlike standard RAG systems designed for text retrieval with pre-trained LLMs, perturbation retrieval lacks established similarity metrics and requires learning what constitutes relevant context, making differentiable retrieval essential. PT-RAG addresses this through a two-stage pipeline: first, retrieving candidate perturbations K using GenePT embeddings, then adaptively refining the selection through Gumbel-Softmax discrete sampling conditioned on both the cell state and the input perturbation. This cell-type-aware differentiable retrieval enables end-to-end optimization of the retrieval objective jointly with generation. On the Replogle-Nadig single-gene perturbation dataset, we demonstrate that PT-RAG outperforms both STATE and vanilla RAG under identical experimental conditions, with the strongest gains in distributional similarity metrics (W_1, W_2). Notably, vanilla RAG's dramatic failure is itself a key finding: it demonstrates that differentiable, cell-type-aware retrieval is essential in this domain, and that naive retrieval can actively harm performance. Our results establish retrieval-augmented generation as a promising paradigm for modelling cellular responses to gene perturbation. The code to reproduce our experiments is available at https://github.com/difra100/PT-RAG_ICLR.
affiliations: ISTI-CNR, Institute of Information Science and Technologies

ru.title: Умный поиск для предсказания клеточных ответов на генетические возмущения
ru.desc: В работе представлен фреймворк PT-RAG для предсказания ответа клеток на генетические возмущения путём комбинирования дифференцируемого поиска с учётом типа клетки и генеративного моделирования. Фреймворк использует двухэтапный подход: сначала извлекаются候补 возмущения через эмбеддинги GenePT, затем производится адаптивная их фильтрация через дискретное семплирование Gumbel-Softmax с учётом состояния клетки и входного возмущения. Экспериментальные результаты на датасете Replogle-Nadig показывают, что PT-RAG превосходит существующие методы, особенно по метрикам распределительной схожести W_1 и W_2. Полученные результаты демонстрируют, что дифференцируемый поиск с учётом типа клетки является критически важным для моделирования клеточных ответов на генные возмущения.

en.title: Revolutionizing Cellular Response Predictions with PT-RAG
en.desc: The PT-RAG framework enhances the prediction of how cells react to genetic changes by integrating a differentiable, cell-type-aware retrieval system with generative modeling. This approach addresses the limitations of previous deep learning methods that struggled to generalize across different cell types and perturbation contexts. By employing a two-stage pipeline, PT-RAG first retrieves relevant perturbations and then refines these selections based on the specific cell state and perturbation input. The results show that PT-RAG significantly outperforms existing methods, highlighting the importance of context-aware retrieval in accurately modeling cellular responses.

zh.title: PT-RAG：细胞反应预测的新范式
zh.desc: PT-RAG框架通过结合可微分的、细胞类型感知的检索与生成建模，提升了对基因扰动的细胞反应预测能力，超越了现有方法在分布相似性指标上的表现。该框架采用两阶段管道，首先使用GenePT嵌入检索候选扰动，然后通过Gumbel-Softmax离散采样根据细胞状态和输入扰动自适应地优化选择。PT-RAG的可微分检索使得检索目标与生成过程能够端到端优化，解决了传统方法在细胞类型和扰动上下文中的泛化问题。我们的实验结果表明，PT-RAG在单基因扰动数据集上优于STATE和普通RAG，尤其在分布相似性指标上取得了显著提升。

papers.title: SeedPolicy: Horizon Scaling via Self-Evolving Diffusion Policy for Robot Manipulation
papers.abstract: Abstract Self-Evolving Gated Attention enables efficient temporal modeling in diffusion policies for long-horizon robotic manipulation, achieving superior performance with reduced computational requirements.  					AI-generated summary Imitation Learning (IL) enables robots to acquire manipulation skills from expert demonstrations. Diffusion Policy (DP) models multi-modal expert behaviors but suffers performance degradation as observation horizons increase, limiting long-horizon manipulation. We propose Self-Evolving Gated Attention (SEGA), a temporal module that maintains a time-evolving latent state via gated attention, enabling efficient recurrent updates that compress long-horizon observations into a fixed-size representation while filtering irrelevant temporal information. Integrating SEGA into DP yields Self-Evolving Diffusion Policy (SeedPolicy), which resolves the temporal modeling bottleneck and enables scalable horizon extension with moderate overhead. On the RoboTwin 2.0 benchmark with 50 manipulation tasks, SeedPolicy outperforms DP and other IL baselines. Averaged across both CNN and Transformer backbones, SeedPolicy achieves 36.8% relative improvement in clean settings and 169% relative improvement in randomized challenging settings over the DP. Compared to vision-language-action models such as RDT with 1.2B parameters, SeedPolicy achieves competitive performance with one to two orders of magnitude fewer parameters, demonstrating strong efficiency and scalability. These results establish SeedPolicy as a state-of-the-art imitation learning method for long-horizon robotic manipulation. Code is available at: https://github.com/Youqiang-Gui/SeedPolicy.

ru.title: Эффективное обучение на примерах для манипуляции роботами через самоэволюционирующее внимание
ru.desc: Статья предлагает Self-Evolving Gated Attention (SEGA) — новый модуль для эффективного моделирования временных зависимостей в диффузионных моделях политик для робототехники. SEGA использует механизм управляемого внимания с эволюционирующимся латентным состоянием для сжатия длинных последовательностей наблюдений в фиксированное представление, одновременно отфильтровывая нерелевантную информацию. Интеграция SEGA в Diffusion Policy создаёт Self-Evolving Diffusion Policy (SeedPolicy), которая значительно улучшает производительность при расширении горизонта планирования с минимальными вычислительными затратами. На бенчмарке RoboTwin 2.0 метод показал 36.8% улучшение в стандартных условиях и 169% в сложных условиях, при этом используя в 100-1000 раз меньше параметров, чем сравнимые видео-языковые модели.

en.title: Revolutionizing Robotic Manipulation with Efficient Temporal Modeling
en.desc: This paper introduces Self-Evolving Gated Attention (SEGA), a novel temporal module designed to enhance the performance of diffusion policies in long-horizon robotic manipulation tasks. SEGA utilizes gated attention to maintain a dynamic latent state, allowing it to efficiently process and compress long observation sequences into a manageable fixed-size representation. By integrating SEGA into the diffusion policy framework, the authors present the Self-Evolving Diffusion Policy (SeedPolicy), which significantly improves temporal modeling and scalability. Experimental results on the RoboTwin 2.0 benchmark demonstrate that SeedPolicy outperforms existing methods while using fewer parameters, showcasing its efficiency and effectiveness in imitation learning.

zh.title: 自演化扩散策略：高效的长时间机器人操作
zh.desc: 自演化门控注意力（SEGA）是一种时间模块，能够通过门控注意力维持时间演变的潜在状态，从而实现高效的递归更新。它将长时间观察压缩为固定大小的表示，同时过滤掉不相关的时间信息。将SEGA集成到扩散策略（DP）中，形成自演化扩散策略（SeedPolicy），有效解决了时间建模瓶颈，支持可扩展的时间范围延伸。实验结果表明，SeedPolicy在RoboTwin 2.0基准测试中表现优异，显著超越了传统的DP和其他模仿学习基线。

papers.title: ByteFlow: Language Modeling through Adaptive Byte Compression without a Tokenizer
papers.abstract: Abstract ByteFlow Net presents a tokenizer-free hierarchical architecture that enables language models to learn adaptive segmentation of raw byte streams through compression-driven methods while maintaining a static computation graph.  					AI-generated summary Modern language models still rely on fixed, pre-defined subword tokenizations. Once a tokenizer is trained, the LM can only operate at this fixed level of granularity, which often leads to brittle and counterintuitive behaviors even in otherwise strong reasoning models. We introduce ByteFlow Net, a new hierarchical architecture that removes tokenizers entirely and instead enables models to learn their own segmentation of raw byte streams into semantically meaningful units. ByteFlow Net performs compression-driven segmentation based on the coding rate of latent representations, yielding adaptive boundaries while preserving a static computation graph via Top-K selection. Unlike prior self-tokenizing methods that depend on brittle heuristics with human-designed inductive biases, ByteFlow Net adapts its internal representation granularity to the input itself. Experiments demonstrate that this compression-based chunking strategy yields substantial performance gains, with ByteFlow Net outperforming both BPE-based Transformers and previous byte-level architectures. These results suggest that end-to-end, tokenizer-free modeling is not only feasible but also more effective, opening a path toward more adaptive and information-grounded language models.

ru.title: Адаптивная сегментация без токенизаторов через компрессионное обучение
ru.desc: ByteFlow Net представляет новую архитектуру языковой модели, которая полностью отказывается от предварительно обученных токенизаторов. Вместо использования фиксированного разделения текста на подсловные единицы, модель самостоятельно обучается сегментировать потоки байтов на семантически значимые единицы через метод сжатия данных. Алгоритм использует Top-K отбор для сохранения статического графа вычислений, адаптивно определяя границы сегментации на основе кодовой скорости скрытых представлений. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что такой подход без токенизатора превосходит традиционные методы с BPE и предыдущие байт-уровневые архитектуры, демонстрируя большую эффективность и гибкость.

en.title: Revolutionizing Language Models: Tokenizer-Free Learning with ByteFlow Net
en.desc: ByteFlow Net is a novel hierarchical architecture that eliminates the need for traditional tokenizers in language models. Instead of relying on fixed subword tokenizations, it allows models to learn adaptive segmentation of raw byte streams based on compression-driven methods. This approach enables the model to dynamically determine meaningful units of text while maintaining a static computation graph. Experimental results show that ByteFlow Net significantly outperforms existing methods, indicating that tokenizer-free modeling can lead to more effective and adaptable language models.

zh.title: 无分词器的自适应语言模型
zh.desc: ByteFlow Net是一种无分词器的层次结构，允许语言模型通过基于压缩的方法学习原始字节流的自适应分割。与传统的固定子词分词方法不同，ByteFlow Net能够根据输入数据自适应地调整内部表示的粒度。该方法通过潜在表示的编码率进行压缩驱动的分割，保持静态计算图并实现更高效的性能。实验结果表明，ByteFlow Net在性能上超越了基于BPE的变换器和之前的字节级架构，展示了无分词器建模的可行性和有效性。
